data.title: Head of Creative Strategy
data.jd_text: About Us: We’re building an ecosystem of digital products and services that power everyday life across the Middle East—fast, scalable, and deeply customer-centric. Our mission is to ring every doorbell, everyday. We want to redefine what technology can do in this region, and we’re looking for a Head of Creative Strategy who can help us move even faster. What you’ll do: The Creative Strategy function sits at the core of how noon shows up to customers — driving high-impact campaigns, creative execution, and brand storytelling across all markets and platforms. This role requires someone who can own end-to-end creative output, from ideation through to execution, and translate business priorities into campaigns that drive growth. You’ll work closely with the CEO, acting as a creative operator, strategist, and force multiplier. You will lead campaign development, shape creative direction, and ensure execution across digital, media, and on-ground channels is best-in-class. This role is built for someone with real creative leadership instinct, sharp thinking, and the ability to operate in fast-paced, high-pressure environments. You should be able to develop strong campaign ideas independently and drive them through to execution with high standards. Key responsibilities include: Lead the development of breakthrough campaign ideas-from concept through to execution Own end-to-end campaign delivery across digital ads, media, social, and key commercial moments Translate business priorities into high-impact creative platforms that drive measurable outcomes Drive hero campaigns across key moments (Ramadan, sale events, growth pushes) Oversee creative production including ad films, digital content, and campaign assets Bring strong filmmaking sensibility — storytelling, pacing, and visual direction Ensure high-quality, fast-moving, performance-driven execution across all channels Shape and evolve noon’s creative direction and tone of voice Drive execution across ATL, BTL, digital, and on-ground activations Work closely with internal teams, agencies, and production partners to deliver at scale Partner with CEO / GrowCo and business leaders on high-priority growth initiatives Build and lead a culture of creative excellence, ownership, and high standards What We’re Looking For: 10–15+ years of experience in creative strategy, advertising, or campaign leadership roles Proven track record of delivering high-impact, full-funnel campaigns Strong creative instinct and independent ideation ability Deep understanding of campaign development across digital, performance, and brand marketing Strong production and filmmaking sensibility Comfortable operating both strategically and hands-on Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments Strong interpersonal skills — able to work across creative, marketing, product, and leadership teams High ownership — able to drive ideas from concept to execution without drop-off Comfortable working under pressure with tight timelines and high expectations Detail-oriented, with a strong focus on quality and output Commercially aware — understands how creativity drives performance and growth Bonus Points If You Have: Experience in e-commerce, tech, or digital-first businesses Strong portfolio of ad films, campaigns, or large-scale creative work Experience working across multiple markets or culturally diverse audiences Prior experience working closely with founders or CEO offices Who will excel? We’re looking for people with high creative standards who understand that great work requires both ideas and execution. You need to be relentlessly resourceful, with a strong bias for action and the ability to move quickly from concept to output. We value individuals with the confidence to push bold ideas, challenge thinking, and raise the bar on creative quality. Success here requires speed, resilience, and the ability to operate in ambiguity while delivering consistently high-quality work.

data.title: Head of Revenue Analytics
data.jd_text:
The Apex Group was established in Bermuda in 2003 and is now one of the world’s largest fund administration and middle office solutions providers.

Our business is unique in its ability to reach globally, service locally and provide cross-jurisdictional services. With our clients at the heart of everything we do, our hard-working team has successfully delivered on an unprecedented growth and transformation journey, and we are now represented by over circa 13,000 employees across 112 offices worldwide.Your career with us should reflect your energy and passion.

That’s why, at Apex Group, we will do more than simply ‘empower’ you. We will work to supercharge your unique skills and experience.

Take the lead and we’ll give you the support you need to be at the top of your game. And we offer you the freedom to be a positive disrupter and turn big ideas into bold, industry-changing realities.

For our business, for clients, and for you

Head of Revenue Analytics

Role Purpose

The Head of Revenue Analytics is responsible for building, owning, and governing Apex Group’s global commercial analytics capability, ensuring trusted, actionable, and forward-looking insights drive revenue predictability and growth.

This role owns the revenue analytics layer across Salesforce and the enterprise data platform, while working in close partnership with Apex’s Global Analytics function and AI teams to integrate broader data sources, advanced analytics, and predictive insights into the commercial operating model.

The role requires a leader who is technically credible, commercially fluent, and able to bridge enterprise data strategy with frontline sales execution—ensuring analytics move from reporting to decisioning and action.

Key Responsibilities

 Revenue Analytics Ownership & ArchitectureOwn the end-to-end revenue analytics domain, including data models, metrics, dashboards, and insight delivery for Sales and CRM.Lead the design of scalable revenue data models across pipeline, forecasting, bookings, and performance.Own the semantic layer and metric definitions that power executive reporting and operational decision-making.Partner with IT and Data Engineering to ensure performance, security, and scalability of the revenue analytics stack. Partnership with Global Analytics FunctionAct as the commercial analytics lead interfacing with Apex’s global enterprise analytics team.Align revenue analytics requirements with enterprise data standards, tooling, and governance.Leverage central analytics capabilities (data science, advanced modeling, data engineering) while retaining clear ownership of revenue-specific outputs.Ensure consistency between enterprise-wide analytics initiatives and commercial reporting. Advanced & Predictive AnalyticsLead the evolution from descriptive and diagnostic reporting to predictive and prescriptive analytics.Develop models to forecast pipeline risk, deal slippage, win probability, and revenue confidence.Partner with AI and data science teams to:Embed predictive insights into Salesforce and commercial workflowsEnable next-best-action recommendations for Sales and CRMSurface risks and opportunities proactively, not reactivelyChampion experimentation and iterative improvement of analytical models. Data Integration & External Data EnablementDrive the integration of additional internal and third-party data sources, including:Market and industry dataClient and fund-level intelligenceBenchmarking and competitive insightsAlternative and external signals relevant to revenue generationEnsure third-party data is mapped, governed, and made actionable within commercial tools.Work with AI and product teams to operationalise insights rather than leaving them in dashboards. Analytics Build & Insight DeliveryLead the build of executive dashboards and scorecards for:Forecast accuracy and confidencePipeline health and conversionSales productivity and performanceRegional, product, and segment trendsEnsure insights are intuitive, timely, and aligned to commercial decision points.Support QBRs, MBRs, and board-level reporting with defensible, consistent analytics. Data Governance, Quality & TrustOwn revenue data governance, including definitions, standards, lineage, and documentation.Establish monitoring and controls for data quality, completeness, and integrity.Partner with Sales Ops, Deal Desk, and CRM to drive upstream data discipline.Serve as the escalation point for revenue data discrepancies across regions. Leadership & Capability DevelopmentBuild and lead a high-performing Revenue Analytics team.Set standards for analytical rigor, documentation, and technical excellence.Develop talent capable of operating confidently across data, AI, and commercial stakeholders.
Required Experience & Profile

Must-Have

10–15+ years in analytics, data, or revenue intelligence roles.Experience leading analytics in financial services, fund administration, fintech, or regulated B2B environments.Strong hands-on background in data model

data.title: Chief Executive Officer
data.jd_text: Strategic Partnership Opportunity – Livbnb x Livstate Context In light of the ongoing economic and geopolitical pressures across the Middle East, many holiday home operators in Dubai are facing unprecedented challenges. Rather than scaling down or shutting operations, Livbnb is extending a strategic opportunity for CEOs and business owners to transition, stabilize, and continue generating revenue through partnership. About the Opportunity Livbnb is opening its full operational infrastructure to select holiday home companies. This is not a traditional job role it is a strategic business collaboration designed to preserve value, sustain income, and create long-term alignment. Through this initiative, partner companies can onboard their existing units into Livbnb’s ecosystem, allowing Livbnb to take over end-to-end management, including operations, marketing, guest experience, and revenue optimization. What Livbnb Offers Full integration into Livbnb’s established platform and operational systems Immediate continuity of income without the burden of day-to-day management A structured revenue-sharing model, ensuring partners retain a percentage of ongoing earnings Retention within the Livbnb ecosystem via Livstate , as a Business Development Partner Role of the CEO / Partner Transition existing holiday home units into Livbnb’s management portfolio Maintain a strategic relationship with Livbnb through Livstate Support expansion by identifying and onboarding additional units where possible Act as a long-term stakeholder in the performance of transitioned assets Broker Incentive Structure Individuals or entities who facilitate the onboarding of units into Livbnb will receive a fixed percentage share of the revenue generated from those units for as long as Livbnb continues to operate them. This creates a recurring income model aligned with performance and longevity. Why This Matters Now This moment represents a critical inflection point for smaller operators. Instead of absorbing operational losses or exiting the market, this partnership enables: Business continuity without operational strain Ongoing revenue participation Alignment with a scalable and resilient platform A pathway to remain active in the industry under a stronger umbrella Positioning This is not an exit it is a transition into a more sustainable and collaborative operating model. Conclusion Livbnb is not acquiring businesses it is building a network. A network of partners who continue to benefit from their assets, relationships, and market knowledge, while leveraging Livbnb’s infrastructure to navigate uncertain times. For CEOs and operators looking for stability, continuity, and upside this is the moment to pivot, not pause.

data.title: Vice President, Digital Product and E-Commerce (Based in Hong Kong)

online spend/tier tracking, etc, as well as supporting efforts to pass relevant guest data points to operational-level systems for personalisation capabilities with on-property colleagu es.Lead future digital integrations across Peninsula’s business units including but not limited to deeper connectivity between hotel and merchandising E-commerce syste ms.Oversee and evolve key relationships with global digital agency partners who support Peninsula with outsourced design and development effor ts.Monitor industry trends and emerging technologies that can support Peninsula’s digital transformation including practical use of AI for both internal productivity as well as guest facing personalisati on.Provide data-driven insights to executive leadership on digital commerce expenditur es. We are delighted to receive your CV for further considera tion.

data.title: Director - IT Business Solutions
data.jd_text: Job Summary : Direct and manage IT business solutions to ensure seamless alignment with organizational digital strategy. Lead the design, delivery and optimization of business applications supporting operational efficiency, customer experience, and revenue growth. Collaborate with business and technology leaders to translate operational needs into scalable IT solutions. Key Accountabilities: Strategic Leadership: Lead the development, implementation, and governance of IT business solutions. Manage the application lifecycle including design, testing, deployment, and post-implementation support. Align business and technology strategies to drive innovation and process optimization. Oversee vendor and stakeholder relationships to ensure timely delivery and service quality. Support digital transformation and automation initiatives aligned with the company's roadmap. Ensure compliance with IT governance, cybersecurity, and data protection standards. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to evaluate business requirements and recommend suitable technology solutions. Drive continuous improvement through analytics, system performance monitoring, and end-user feedback. Define the multi-year technology roadmap for the business, aligning it with company’s broader digital and business objectives. Technology Enablement: Lead the evaluation and implementation of ERP, CRM, IoT, and data analytics solutions. Compliance & Security: Ensure all IT solutions comply with corporate governance, cybersecurity, and data protection policies. Education, Experience and Skills: Essential Qualification: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field. Desirable Qualification: Master’s degree in IT or Business Management. Experience: Minimum 10–12 years in IT business solution management, with 5+ years in leadership roles managing enterprise applications or transformation projects. Skills: Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills Proven expertise in solution delivery and digital transformation Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities Strategic thinking with operational execution capability

data.title: IT & Systems Director
data.jd_text: IT & Systems Director - on behalf of a client LOCATION : DIFC, Dubai SALARY : Depending on experience ABOUT US We are launching a brand-new and exclusive members' club, located in the heart of Dubai, that offers a private world of unparalleled connections. Representing a new pinnacle of sophistication and elegance in the region, the club sets itself apart as a carefully curated membership offering, fusing Emirati cultural nuance with timeless heritage hospitality. We are committed to providing exceptional service and creating unforgettable experiences for our distinguished members. ROLE OVERVEW The IT & Systems Director holds executive responsibility for the club’s technology ecosystem, ensuring all digital platforms, infrastructure and operational systems function as a secure, resilient and fully integrated environment. This includes CRM, POS, finance systems, member-facing platforms, access control and data governance. Reporting to the Club General Manager, the role ensures technology supports the full member lifecycle while enabling efficient operations and high service standards across the club. The IT & Systems Director oversees systems procurement, integration and vendor management, ensuring platforms operate as a coherent and scalable architecture aligned with the club’s membership strategy, privacy standards and long-term growth. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES System Strategy & Architecture Own the end-to-end systems architecture, with Salesforce as the core CRM and system of record . Define how all digital and operational systems connect, interact and evolve over time. Ensure technology supports the full member lifecycle, from first introduction to long-term retention. Establish clear principles for privacy, data governance, security and system ownership across the stack. Ensure infrastructure resilience, cybersecurity standards and operational continuity across all systems. Salesforce & Core Platform Ownership Lead the i n-house implementation, configuration and ongoing ownership of Salesforce . Design Salesforce architecture to support: Prospecting, referrals and provenance tracking, Applications, committee workflows and approvals, KYC, onboarding and founder engagement, Member engagement, usage and retention. Act as internal product owner, translating operational and commercial needs into Salesforce delivery. Configure objects, workflows, automations, dashboards and reporting aligned to the approved membership operating model. Systems Integration & Delivery Oversee integration of Salesforce with all core systems, including: Website and application flows, Member app and portal, POS, reservations and table management, Access control and identity systems, Payments and finance. Ensure integrations are stable, well-documented and future-proofed. Minimise dependency on external vendors by retaining system knowledge in-house. Selectively manage specialist external partners where required, with clear accountability and quality control. Ensure alignment between CRM, POS, finance and operational systems to maintain a unified data environment. Manage technology vendors and implementation partners, ensuring performance, accountability and knowledge retention in-house. Pre-opening Implementation Lead delivery of a fully integrated systems environment ready for soft opening. Ensure all systems are tested end-to-end against real operational scenarios. Align systems delivery tightly with the pre-opening critical path. Enable founder onboarding, early bookings and pre-opening engagement through digital channels. Live Operations & Optimisation Own systems performance once the club is live, ensuring reliability, data quality and operational clarity. Continually optimise systems based on member behaviour, staff feedback and operational learnings. Support Membership, Brand, Operations and Finance teams with insight, reporting and system-driven decision-making. Introduce phased enhancements to automation, personalisation and intelligence over time. Build internal documentation, training and handover processes to ensure long-term resilience. SALESFORCE EXPERIENCE Please note, this role requires deep Salesforce experience and applications without this will not be considered. While formal certification is not a substitute for real-world delivery, the successful candidate is expected to demonstrate strong Salesforce platform expertise, supported by relevant credentials or equivalent hands-on experience. Candidates should have personally led Salesforce delivery within complex, live operational environments. REQUIREMENTS Must be currently based in Dubai or available to relocate to Dubai for a full-time role. 10+ experience in senior IT, systems or digital leadership roles within hospitality, private clubs, luxury environments or comparable service-led organisations. Deep, hands-on experience owning Salesforce implementations in-house, from architecture and configuration through to live operation and optimisation. Demonstrated abilit

data.title: Director of Data Science
data.jd_text: About the Company Careem is building the Everything App for the greater Middle East — making it easy to move around, order food and groceries, manage payments, and more. Our purpose is simple: to simplify and improve people’s lives and build an awesome organisation that inspires. Since 2012, Careem has enabled earnings for over 2.5 million Captains, simplified the lives of more than 70 million customers, and built a platform where the region’s best talent and entrepreneurs thrive. We operate in 70+ cities across 10 countries, from Morocco to Pakistan. We’re now entering our next chapter — one powered by AI. We’re looking for AI talent: curious problem-solvers who know how to apply AI to build tools, automate workflows, and create real impact. Whether it’s streamlining operations, enhancing customer experience, or reimagining internal systems — we want people who can make Careem work smarter and move faster. About The Role The Director of Data Science (ML) leads Careem’s central Machine Learning and Data Science organization — a 30+ members team driving applied AI innovation across the Everything App. This leader defines and executes Careem’s AI strategy, scaling machine learning capabilities across all verticals, and ensuring AI becomes a core competitive differentiator. You will shape how Careem personalizes user experiences, optimizes its marketplace dynamics, and leverages the latest in Generative AI and Large Language Models to serve millions of customers every day. What You'll Do 1. Strategic Leadership Define and execute the vision and multi-year strategy for Data Science and Machine Learning across all Careem verticals. Closely partner with product, engineering, and business leaders to identify and prioritize high-impact AI opportunities with measurable business ROI. Drive standardization, scalability, and platformization across ML systems to accelerate delivery and innovation. Evangelize AI across the company, ensuring alignment between AI investments and Careem’s overall strategic priorities. 2. Team Leadership & Development Build, lead, and inspire a high-performing team of 30+ ML engineers and data scientists distributed across geographies and domains. Foster a culture of inclusion, experimentation, and excellence — empowering teams to take ownership and innovate. Ensure clear ownership and accountability across core DS streams, and continuously evolve team structure for scale and accelerating impact. Mentor emerging leaders and invest in developing technical and leadership capabilities across the organization. 3. Technical & Operational Excellence Oversee the design, development, and deployment of end-to-end ML systems — from data collection and feature engineering to productionized models and monitoring. Partner with engineering to ensure robust infrastructure for training, retraining, and serving ML models at scale. Define and oversee the technical AI roadmap and direction for the team. Lead innovation in Generative AI and LLM applications, ensuring Careem stays at the forefront of applied AI technologies. Champion MLOps and automation to reduce experimentation-to-production cycle time. 4. Product Impact & Delivery Ensure each Data Science domain delivers measurable impact aligned with company OKRs and product outcomes. Collaborate with vertical leaders to embed ML into user-facing experiences (e.g., personalized discovery, dynamic ranking, ETA prediction, integrity systems). Establish clear success metrics and measurement frameworks for AI-driven initiatives with clear AI impact accounting. Champion a data-driven culture across Careem — ensuring insights and experimentation inform every product and business decision. 5. Cross-Functional Influence Represent Data Science leadership in executive and strategic forums, influencing company priorities and investment decisions. Collaborate closely with Engineering Platform, Product Analytics, and Data Platform teams to align on architecture, tooling, and data strategy. Serve as a thought leader for AI innovation across the region and strengthen collaboration with academia and research centers. What You'll Need 10+ years of experience in Data Science, ML, or Applied AI, including 5+ years leading large, high-performing technical teams. Proven record of building and scaling ML systems in production for large-scale consumer platforms (e.g., personalization, recommendation, search, marketplace optimization, fraud detection). Advanced degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related field. Demonstrated ability to translate complex ML concepts into strategic business impact. Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across technical and non-technical domains. Deep passion for product-oriented AI — using data and ML to create delightful, human-centered experiences. What We’ll Provide You We offer colleagues the opportunity to drive impact in the region while they learn and grow. As a full time Careem colleague, you will be able to: Work

data.title: Head of Faculty – Art & Design Technology – August 2026 start
data.jd_text:
About Us:

Fortes Education?has been a leading education provider in the UAE for over four decades with premium international schools and nurseries that have experienced outstanding success and growth. Our schools offer the most innovative and character-building education in the entire Middle East region.

Our core ethos and philosophy of education is Positive Education (PosEd). Through PosEd, our schools foster active engagement in learning,?celebrating character strengths and accomplishments. We encourage children to cultivate fulfilling relationships with others and seek meaning and purpose in their own lives.?We enable children to become happy learners and develop into innovative thinkers, confident young adults, and life-long learners. Our students develop resilience, a growth mindset and deep character strengths that allow them to flourish in an ever-changing world.?

About Sunmarke School –?Where Amazing Happens

Sunmarke School provides an ambitious and inspiring, values-based education that nurtures the hearts and minds of students so that they can flourish and achieve their dreams. Powered by our suite of signature programmes, Sunmarke is a uniquely innovative school, transforming young lives.?This was reflected in Sunmarke School winning the coveted ‘Most Innovative School’ award at the international Which School Advisor ceremony in Dubai and London.

Sunmarke is an all–through school which offers a premium education across a fantastic range of pathways for students, including the National Curriculum of England, GCSE/IGCSE,?‘A’?Levels, BTEC and the IB Diploma Programmes.??Our students regularly win examination boards’ ‘best in world’, ‘best in Middle East’ and ‘best in country’ awards every year, across a diverse range of subjects. The latest incredible IB results show that Sunmarke School is in the top 3 schools in Dubai and 69th in world rankings. Our suite of Signature Programmes is unrivalled in Dubai, with inspiring opportunities for students to experience transformative learning opportunities. These include Environmental and Sustainability Studies, where students learn in Forest School settings, as well as utilising cutting-edge hydroponic technology to examine how science can support sustainability in future terrains; a world-class STEAM centre and curriculum, which offers students an incredible opportunity to apply their learning in solving real-world problems, from Foundation Stage through to Year 9; Augmented and Virtual Reality AR/VR experiences alongside our cutting edge use of Artificial Intelligence for staff and students; outstanding performing arts opportunities with full black box and auditorium facilities alongside peripatetic music spaces and media suites; and a fantastic sports programme augmented with professional coaches, which led Sunmarke to be overall champions at the BSME Games.?

Sunmarke School has been judged by the Dubai School Inspection Bureau (DSIB) as a?‘Very Good’?school since our third?year of operation and is consistently judged ‘Outstanding’ by British?Schools Overseas (BSO).

We present an exciting opportunity to join our all through school and to play an important part in our journey beyond?“outstanding”. Sunmarke School, Dubai is one of our flagship schools, located at?a prime location in Jumeirah Village Triangle.?

We are seeking an exceptional and inspirational Head of Faculty – Art & Design Technology to lead and shape a vibrant, innovative department across all key stages. This is a teaching and middle leadership role, and the successful candidate will maintain an active teaching commitment across Art and Design Technology, delivering outstanding lessons while driving creativity, innovation, and excellence across the faculty.

About You:

We are looking for a leader who:

data.title: Head Of Product Design
data.jd_text: Job Description 1inch is the DeFi ecosystem building financial freedom for everyone. 1inch products help users and builders trade, hold and track digital assets - with the self-custody, comprehensive security and the intuitive user experience they need to unlock the potential of true crypto ownership. 1inch protocols and APIs provide core infrastructure across the DeFi industry and beyond. So if you’re someone who thinks big, moves fast and wants to make an impact right from day one, then get ready to join our industry-changing team. Location: We prioritise candidates who are based in Dubai then consider those working remotely in one of our European Hub Locations (UK, Spain or Germany). As Head of Product Design, you will shape the future of user experience at 1inch by driving the vision, strategy, and execution of design across all our products in crypto, blockchain, and DeFi. You are a key leader aligning design with business goals and technology innovation, influencing product roadmaps at the executive level and inspiring teams to deliver world-class, user-centered experiences. You will ensure validated, end-to-end UX flows are delivered ahead of launch milestones, using data, experimentation, and user insight to reduce late-stage UX changes and accelerate delivery. Design under your leadership directly supports revenue growth through higher trading conversion, improved retention, and a cohesive, scalable product experience. By strengthening design systems and cross-functional collaboration, you help reduce UX-related support tickets and raise the overall quality bar across platforms. This role requires a strategic mindset, strong leadership, deep Web3 and crypto UX expertise, and the ability to operate seamlessly across product, engineering, marketing, and business teams. Key Responsibilities Lead long-term UX planning across 1inch products, defining a multi-year design direction while delivering fast, incremental improvements that balance innovation, scalability, and reliable execution Own UX readiness for product launches, ensuring validated end-to-end user flows are delivered ahead of engineering milestones and late-stage UX changes are minimized Ensure a cohesive, user-centric product experience at scale through strong design system adoption Oversee the evolution of the design system, brand consistency, and accessibility standards across all platforms Manage and grow a team of Product Designers, including hiring, mentorship, performance management, and long-term career development Requirements 7+ years of progressive experience in UX/UI/Product Design leadership roles, ideally in crypto, fintech, or complex tech ecosystems Proven track record of managing and scaling design teams delivering cross-platform products (web, iOS, Android) Proven ownership of design systems and scalable UX foundations across multiple products Demonstrated ability to drive measurable business outcomes (conversion, retention, revenue) through design Deep understanding of UX principles, interaction design, accessibility, and branding within blockchain/Web3 context Strong strategic thinking with the ability to balance visionary leadership and operational execution Excellent communication and storytelling skills for diverse audiences, including executives and technical teams Fluent English communication skills (written and spoken), with the ability to clearly articulate complex design concepts to executive and technical audiences Nice to Have Background in cryptocurrency DeFi and blockchain ecosystems UX or product design certifications (e.g. Nielsen Norman Group) are a plus Why work for us? Join a young, creative team in a fast-paced and supportive environment We’re open to new ideas — if you’ve got a vision, pitch it and make it happen Enjoy competitive pay that matches your skills and experience Be part of a company that’s shaping the future of DeFi Take time when you need it — we offer unlimited vacation days Get fully compensated for your work gear — we’ll set you up for success We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Design under your leadership directly supports revenue growth through higher trading conversion, improved retention, and a cohesive, scalable product experience. By strengthening design systems and cross-functional collaboration, you help reduce UX-related support tickets and raise the overall quality bar across platforms. This role requires a strategic mindset, strong leadership, deep Web3 and crypto UX expertise, and the ability to operate seamlessly across product, engineering, marketing, and business teams. Key Responsibilities Lead long-term UX planning across 1inch products, defining a multi-year design direction while delivering fast, incremental improvements that balance innovation, scalability, and reliable execution Own UX readiness for product launches, ensuring validated end-to-end user flows are delivered ahead of engineering milestones and late-stage UX changes are minimized Ensure a cohesive, user-centric product experience at scale through strong design system adoption Oversee the evolution of the design system, brand consistency, and accessibility standards across all platforms Manage and grow a team of Product Designers, including hiring, mentorship, performance management, and long-term career development Requirements 7+ years of progressive experience in UX/UI/Product Design leadership roles, ideally in crypto, fintech, or complex tech ecosystems Proven track record of managing and scaling design teams delivering cross-platform products (web, iOS, Android) Proven ownership of design systems and scalable UX foundations across multiple products Demonstrated ability to drive measurable business outcomes (conversion, retention, revenue) through design Deep understanding of UX principles, interaction design, accessibility, and branding within blockchain/Web3 context Strong strategic thinking with the ability to balance visionary leadership and operational execution Excellent communication and storytelling skills for diverse audiences, including executives and technical teams Fluent English communication skills (written and spoken), with the ability to clearly articulate complex design concepts to executive and technical audiences Nice to Have Background in cryptocurrency DeFi and blockchain ecosystems UX or product design certifications (e.g. Nielsen Norman Group) are a plus Why work for us? Join a young, creative team in a fast-paced and supportive environment We’re open to new ideas — if you’ve got a vision, pitch it and make it happen Enjoy competitive pay that matches your skills and experience Be part of a company that’s shaping the future of DeFi Take time when you need it — we offer unlimited vacation days Get fully compensated for your work gear — we’ll set you up for success We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

data.title: Head of Chemistry – August 2026 start
data.jd_text: About us: Fortes Education has been a leading education provider in the UAE for over four decades with premium international schools and nurseries that have experienced outstanding success and growth. Our schools offer the most innovative and character-building education in the entire Middle East region. Our core ethos and philosophy of education is Positive Education (PosEd). Through PosEd, our schools foster active engagement in learning, celebrating character strengths and accomplishments. We encourage children to cultivate fulfilling relationships with others and seek meaning and purpose in their own lives. We enable children to become happy learners and develop into innovative thinkers, confident young adults, and life-long learners. Our students develop resilience, a growth mindset and deep character strengths that allow them to flourish in an ever-changing world. About Sunmarke School – Where Amazing Happens Sunmarke School provides an ambitious and inspiring, values-based education that nurtures the hearts and minds of students so that they can flourish and achieve their dreams. Powered by our suite of signature programmes, Sunmarke is a uniquely innovative school, transforming young lives. This was reflected in Sunmarke School winning the coveted ‘Most Innovative School’ award at the international Which School Advisor ceremony in Dubai and London. Sunmarke is an all–through school which offers a premium education across a fantastic range of pathways for students, including the National Curriculum of England, GCSE/IGCSE, ‘A’ Levels, BTEC and the IB Diploma Programmes. Our students regularly win examination boards’ ‘best in world’, ‘best in Middle East’ and ‘best in country’ awards every year, across a diverse range of subjects. The latest incredible IB results show that Sunmarke School is in the top 3 schools in Dubai and 69th in world rankings. Our suite of Signature Programmes is unrivalled in Dubai, with inspiring opportunities for students to experience transformative learning opportunities. These include Environmental and Sustainability Studies, where students learn in Forest School settings, as well as utilising cutting-edge hydroponic technology to examine how science can support sustainability in future terrains; a world-class STEAM centre and curriculum, which offers students an incredible opportunity to apply their learning in solving real-world problems, from Foundation Stage through to Year 9; Augmented and Virtual Reality AR/VR experiences alongside our cutting edge use of Artificial Intelligence for staff and students; outstanding performing arts opportunities with full black box and auditorium facilities alongside peripatetic music spaces and media suites; and a fantastic sports programme augmented with professional coaches, which led Sunmarke to be overall champions at the BSME Games. Sunmarke School has been judged by the Dubai School Inspection Bureau (DSIB) as a ‘Very Good’ school since our third year of operation and is consistently judged ‘Outstanding’ by British Schools Overseas (BSO). We present an exciting opportunity to join our all through school and to play an important part in our journey beyond “outstanding”. Sunmarke School, Dubai is one of our flagship schools, located at a prime location in Jumeirah Village Triangle. About you: We seek to appoint an outstanding Head of Chemistry for an August 2026 start. We are looking for certain qualities: Skilled in the best global educational practices Committed to developing and inspiring every student, to believe that they can and will succeed Tech-savvy, fluent in using APPs and technology to extend and enrich each student’s learning experience Outstanding role models who embody our core values Passionate about providing enriched learning opportunities for children in and out of the classroom. Excellent communicators who can ignite a passion for learning in our students. Able to make a significant contribution to our extra-curricular activities (ECA) programme and wider campus life Strong academic background, work ethic, well-organised having excellent interpersonal skills Dynamic, enthusiastic, and well-qualified, holding at minimum a B.Ed. or a B.A. with a PGCE in Chemistry, along with QTS. This opportunity will include teaching the subject at Key Stage 3, I/GCSE, ‘A’ Level, & /or IB Diploma programmes (N.B. experience in all programmes is preferred but not essential). Our Offer: · Excellent tax-free salary · Furnished Accommodation · Relocation Allowance (applicable to overseas appointments only) · Medical Insurance for self · Annual Air-Ticket and paid vacations · Tuition Fee Concession for up to 2 minor dependent(s) · State-of-the-art facilities and amenities in a newly built campus in a prime location in Dubai · An enthusiastic learning community of students, teachers, parents, and Board members, who are dedicated to maximise student achievement, and creating positive learning opportunities in and out of the classroom. ·

data.title: Head of Veterinary Nursing
data.jd_text: The nursing team makes or breaks a veterinary clinic. Not the marketing. Not the technology. Not even the vets. It’s the nurses who set the pace, hold the standard, and determine whether a clinic feels chaotic or calm. We’re looking for someone who knows this — and wants to build a nursing culture from the ground up. A new veterinary group is launching across the UAE with a technology-first, client-obsessed approach to veterinary care — and ambitious plans for rapid expansion into the wider GCC, particularly KSA. This is a founding Head of Veterinary Nursing role — an opportunity to define nursing standards, build the team, and shape the clinical culture across an entire network of clinics. The Role This is a hands-on leadership role. You’ll spend roughly 60% of your time in active clinical practice and 40% on leadership, training, and operational responsibilities. You won’t be managing from behind a desk — you’ll be the most experienced nurse on the floor, leading by example while building the systems and standards around you. You’ll design and implement nursing protocols across every clinic in the network. You’ll recruit, train, and mentor veterinary nurses and technicians — building a team that delivers consistent, compassionate, high-quality care regardless of which location a client walks into. You’ll own the nursing department budget, manage staffing costs, and ensure the nursing function operates efficiently as we scale. You’ll work closely with the Veterinary Clinical Director to ensure nursing and clinical standards are fully aligned, and with the Practice Manager to ensure staffing, scheduling, and workflow run seamlessly. As we scale, every nurse in the network will look to you as the standard-setter and the advocate for their profession. Day to day, you’ll be: Clinical (approx. 60%): Assisting in surgeries — anaesthesia monitoring, patient preparation, surgical support, and post-operative recovery Running nurse-led consultations where appropriate — post-op checks, weight clinics, vaccination appointments, and chronic condition monitoring Performing and processing in-house diagnostics — blood work, urinalysis, cytology, and imaging preparation Managing inpatient care — treatment administration, patient monitoring, and clinical record-keeping Triaging emergency and walk-in cases alongside the veterinary team Leadership (approx. 40%): Designing and implementing nursing protocols, SOPs, and clinical care standards across all clinics Recruiting, onboarding, and training new veterinary nurses and technicians Running regular training sessions, skills assessments, and CPD planning for the nursing team Managing the nursing roster, staffing ratios, and department budget Conducting performance reviews and mentoring junior nurses toward career progression Collaborating with the Clinical Director and Practice Manager on workflow, capacity planning, and quality improvement This will suit you if: You’re a qualified and experienced veterinary nurse (5+ years) with leadership or supervisory experience You want to lead without stepping away from clinical work — you’re at your best when you’re still on the floor You’ve built or significantly improved nursing standards in a previous practice You believe veterinary nursing deserves more recognition, more autonomy, and better career paths — and you want to prove it You’re a natural teacher who gets fulfilment from developing junior nurses into confident professionals You’re comfortable with technology and excited to work with AI-powered tools, digital patient records, and modern practice management systems You’re capable of operating in a fast-paced, high-growth, entrepreneurial environment where things move quickly and you’re expected to lead, not wait You’re based in the GCC, or you’re an exceptional international candidate open to relocation What’s on offer A founding nursing leadership role with genuine influence over clinical culture Competitive salary reflective of the seniority and scope of this position Equity participation through the company’s Long-Term Incentive Plan (LTIP) — a share-based equity programme for the right candidate Relocation support for international candidates — visa sponsorship, housing assistance, and transition support Technology and infrastructure that empowers nurses rather than burdening them with admin The chance to work with a proven team that has already disrupted and scaled 50+ sites across the GCC A clear career path from single-site nursing lead to regional Head of Nursing as we scale across the UAE and into KSA If you’ve ever wanted to define what great veterinary nursing looks like — not just practise within someone else’s version of it — this is your moment. Apply now. All applications are completely confidential. Shortlisted candidates will receive full details on the brand, the technology, the team, and the timeline.

data.title: VP - Engineering (Exchange)
data.jd_text:
The OpportunityCronos Labs is building the infrastructure to collapse every speculative market into one app. Tokenized stocks, crypto perpetuals, prediction markets, spot: one balance, every market, powered by Crypto.com's 150M+ users and regulated infrastructure for tokenized equities.
We need a VP- Engineering to build the trading engine that makes this possible. The core technical mandate is a hybrid onchain Central Limit Order Book (CLOB) and matching engine with institutional-grade risk infrastructure: cross-asset margin, real-time liquidation systems, and a risk engine that can handle correlated drawdowns across tokenized stocks and crypto derivatives simultaneously. This is the single biggest capability gap on the team. Our blockchain and platform engineering is well-staffed and capable. What we lack is the person who has built centralized, high-performance trading infrastructure and can translate that expertise into a hybrid onchain architecture.
This is a greenfield build. You are not inheriting someone else's architecture. The Sidecar Sequencer (Rust, off-chain) handles order matching at <5ms P50 latency and 100K OPS throughput. The Cronos EVM handles settlement and custody. You will own the full technical stack from matching engine to smart contracts to mobile app, with the team across Smart Contracts, Blockchain, DevOps, Platform, QA, Trading, and Inventory.
The Product You Are BuildingCronos App is a mobile-first trading app that lets users:Trade tokenized stocks (NVDA, TSLA, etc.) 24/7 with increased buying powerTrade crypto perpetuals and spotTrade sports and event outcomes (prediction markets) with the ability to exit earlyUse one balance to collateralize positions across all asset classesChoose between "Simple/Fun Mode" (guardrails, plain language) and "Pro Mode" (advanced execution, lowest fees)The product is powered by Crypto.com's regulated infrastructure for tokenized equities and prediction markets and built on the Cronos blockchain.
What You'll OwnPrimary Mandate: Trading Infrastructure (The Gap)
Hybrid CLOB & Matching Engine. Architect and ship the off-chain Sidecar Sequencer (Rust) with <5ms P50 tick-to-trade latency, 100K OPS throughput, and <1ms latency variance. Integrate with Cronos EVM for onchain settlement (<60s settlement delay). Route between onchain CLOB and offchain RFQ for best execution.Risk Engine & Margin Systems. Build the full risk stack: collateral weighting, cross-asset netting, initial margin checks, dynamic maintenance thresholds. Unified margin where tokenized equities can margin crypto trades and vice versa. Real-time risk calculations across stocks, crypto, and prediction markets.Liquidation Infrastructure. Design and implement the liquidation engine, auto-deleveraging (ADL) system, and insurance fund architecture. These must survive correlated market crashes, not just normal volatility. The insurance fund must be verifiable onchain with clear deployment rules.Perpetuals & Derivatives Engine. Perpetual futures with funding rate mechanisms, leverage management, and position lifecycle. Phase 1 forks the CDC Exchange matching engine; Phase 2 transitions to a fully onchain Global Order Book.Oracle Integration & Market Data. Manipulation-resistant price feeds for liquidation triggers, mark price computation, and cross-market reference pricing.
Secondary Mandate: Platform & Blockchain Smart Contract & Blockchain Infrastructure. Cronos chain upgrades, USDC migration (IOU to native stablecoin), bridge and cross-chain infrastructure, smart contract security and audit pipeline. Vault, Clearing House, Insurance Fund, and Governance contracts.Platform & Mobile Engineering. Mobile-first consumer app for emerging markets. Embedded wallet with passkey-based authentication. Account abstraction and TEE/verifiable compute for signing. API Gateway and SDK.DevOps & QA. CI/CD, infrastructure reliability, automated testing, performance benchmarking.CDC Integration & Coordination. Coordinate with the Crypto.com tokenization team on shared Confluence/documentation workflows and align timelines across organizations.
What We're Looking ForRequiredDeep trading infrastructure and risk management experience. You have built matching engines, margin systems, liquidation engines, or real-time risk platforms. You understand order matching semantics, cross-collateralization models, and how liquidation cascades propagate under stress. This experience can come from tier-1 investment banks, hedge funds, prop shops, centralized crypto exchanges, or DeFi derivatives protocols.Derivatives market structure fluency. Perpetuals, options, funding rates, mark price computation, portfolio margin. You don't just understand the theory. You've built systems that handle these instruments at production scale.Hands-on blockchain/DeFi depth or proven ability to ramp fast. EVM smart contracts (Solidity), protocol design, bridge security, token standards. If you're crossing over from TradFi, you need to demonstra

data.title: Commissioning Director
data.jd_text: JDL Recruitment are delighted with a highly reputable Data Centre Developer who are expanding their team with a Commissioning Director to be based in the UAE. Applicant must be willing to relocate to the UAE or already be based in country. Responsibilities Develop and lead the implementation of testing & commissioning and handover plans/strategies that support the programme delivery requirements. Collaborate with programme management, engineering & development, and project teams to ensure construction timelines and methods support future scalability and modular expansion. Create long-term design strategies that align with customer requirements and expectations Champion a proactive collaboration and co-operation culture across the programme through leadership engagement, workforce empowerment, and behavioural initiatives. The development and implementation of programme-wide policies, systems, processes, procedures, and controls for testing & commissioning and handover. The standardisation of document workflows across the programme. Prepare testing & commissioning and hand over elements of the Employer’s Requirements (ERs) for contractors as part of their contract agreements. Participate in and respond to all questionnaires related testing & commissioning and hand over as raised by customers during the RFQ/Bid stage. Provide guidance to the design team to ensure project design captures all necessary testing & commissioning requirements. Participate in consultants and contractors daily, weekly, and monthly project meetings. Conduct regular inspections of on-site work to ensure adherence to good practices, international standards, and design specifications. Define customer requirements to define project technical requirements and objectives related to testing & commissioning and handover Lead the development of project-related testing & commissioning documentation, including Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) and Integrated System Testing (IST) scripts in line with company standards and best practices, ensuring consistency and excellence across the programme. Oversee the review and approval of testing & commissioning method statements and risk assessments Oversee and collaborate with contractors, suppliers, vendors, and internal team members to ensure effective execution of all testing and commissioning tasks to ensure Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve technical issues or faults encountered during the testing and commissioning phases. Resolve on-site conflicts related to testing & commissioning between contractors and technical advisors from a design perspective, proposing the best solutions for the benefit of the company. Act as the main point of contact with customers, leading testing & commissioning discussions and clarifications. Drive HSE leadership among project teams, ensuring integration of HSE in the planning, testing & commissioning, and handover phases. Requirements Relevant bachelor’s degree or equivalent Relevant professional qualification or chartered status 15+ years of relevant professional experience (including a previous director role), including the testing & commissioning of data centers or other complex engineering projects across the entire project life cycle.

data.title: Director of Operations

This position acts as a strategic right hand , responsible for leading key initiatives across high-level stakeholder engagements. You will play a critical role in: Building and managing relationships with senior stakeholders across government, private sector, and philanthropic organisations Leading strategic initiatives, including research, insights, and thought leadership Overseeing high-level convenings such as roundtables, forums, and international engagements Driving external positioning, communications, and stakeholder messaging Managing a small team and ensuring effective execution of priorities We are looking for a senior, credible professional who can operate confidently in a complex and high-level environment. data.title: Director of Operations
data.jd_text: Hays are working with a leading investment firm as they are looking to hire a Director of Operations to work directly with the CEO and oversee key control and operational functions across the business, the role can be based out of the UAE or Bahrain. They are looking for an experience Operational professional with a strong background within the Investment ecosystem. As a key member of the leadership team, the Director of Operations will own the firm’s end‑to‑end operating framework, ensuring strong governance, regulatory discipline, and execution excellence across the platform. Working closely with the CEO, the role will translate strategy into scalable operating processes, providing oversight across compliance, finance, and transaction execution while ensuring the firm’s operating model supports disciplined growth. The position will act as a control point between strategy and execution, strengthening decision‑making, board engagement, and stakeholder confidence, while continuously optimising how the business identifies, executes, and manages investments. Requirements: Bachelors degree in Finance or related field 10+ years of experience in investment management organisations Preferably 7+ years of experience in Big 4 (FS clients only) Strong understanding of the asset management / investment firm operating model and how control functions enable execution at pace Comfortable operating as a senior business partner to the CEO and working across multiple functions with confidence and maturity. The client is offering an attractive salary and benefits for the selected candidate, and rare opportunity to work for a top tier investment firm in the region and report directly to a high regarded and well respected regional CEO. Please follow the link to apply directly, only candidates that meet the above criteria will be contacted to discuss further.

data.title: Managing Director
data.jd_text: Job Title: Managing Director Location: Dubai, United Arab Emirates Industry: Manufacturing Job Summary: We are seeking a visionary and results-driven Managing Director to lead and oversee all aspects of our manufacturing operations in Dubai. The Managing Director will be responsible for driving business growth, ensuring operational efficiency, and executing the company’s long-term strategy. This role requires a dynamic leader with strong commercial acumen, the ability to manage cross-functional teams, and a deep understanding of the manufacturing sector. Key Responsibilities: Develop and implement the company’s overall business strategy to achieve growth objectives and profitability. Oversee daily operations, ensuring optimal performance across production, supply chain, sales, and quality control. Lead and inspire cross-functional teams, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. Drive business development efforts, identify new market opportunities, and expand the company’s presence in the region. Collaborate with the executive team to align business operations with corporate goals and strategic initiatives. Manage financial performance, including budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting to ensure the company meets its targets. Ensure compliance with industry regulations, health and safety standards, and environmental requirements. Build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders, including customers, suppliers, and regulatory authorities. Lead risk management initiatives to safeguard the company’s financial and operational health. Monitor market trends and competitive landscape to stay ahead of industry changes and challenges. Represent the company at industry events and in key business negotiations. Foster a positive and collaborative workplace culture, driving employee engagement and retention. Requirements: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Engineering, or a related field. Proven experience as a Managing Director, General Manager, or in a senior leadership role within the manufacturing sector. Strong understanding of manufacturing operations, supply chain management, and production processes. Demonstrated experience in driving business growth and managing P&L responsibilities. Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and inspire at all levels. Strategic thinker with a proven track record of successfully executing long-term business strategies. Strong financial acumen and experience in managing budgets and financial performance. Ability to navigate complex challenges, lead change, and make data-driven decisions. Knowledge of local and international market trends and regulations in the manufacturing industry. Experience working in the Middle East or familiarity with the UAE business environment is an advantage.

data.title: Systems & Service Operations Director – Controls
data.jd_text:
What You Will Do

The Systems & Service Operations Director for Controls is accountable for operational governance and execution excellence across the Controls Order-to-Cash lifecycle. This role ensures disciplined Percentage of Completion management, predictable backlog-to-invoice conversion, and strong system governance across countries and Controls domains. The position establishes standard work through robust SOPs and provides functional leadership to country and domain systems leaders to ensure consistency, compliance, and cash velocity. The role also defines SOP and governance for pre sales and after sales engineering for EMEA.

Key Responsibilities

How you will do it:

Controls Service Order-to-Cash Governance

Own end-to-end governance of the Controls Order-to-Cash process from order release through execution, revenue recognition, invoicing, and cash realization.Ensure Controls projects are executed in line with contractual milestones, system readiness, and commercial commitments.Act as the single operational authority connecting project execution systems, finance processes, and service delivery.

Percentage of Completion Management

Establish and govern a standardized Percentage of Completion framework for Controls service projects.Ensure accurate progress measurement tied to physical completion, system commissioning, and cost consumption.Drive consistent application of POC rules across countries to eliminate revenue volatility and late recognition.Partner with Finance to ensure compliance with accounting standards and internal controls.

Service Systems Leadership & Country Governance

Provide functional leadership and governance to Country and Domain Controls Service Systems Leaders.Define operating rhythms, review cadences, and escalation paths for system execution risks.Ensure consistent system usage and data quality across Controls service platforms.

Standard Operating Procedures & Standard Work

Design, deploy, and maintain structured SOPs covering all critical Controls service workflows including:Order handoff from Projects to ServiceProgress updates and POC validationChange order and variation handlingInvoicing readiness and releaseDrive adoption of standard work to reduce execution variability across countries and service teams.
Backlog Health & Invoicing Conversion

Monitor Controls service backlog health including aging, stalled projects, and unbilled value.Identify root causes preventing backlog conversion such as system gaps, milestone misalignment, or execution delays.Lead corrective actions to improve invoice timeliness and backlog burn rate.Ensure invoicing reflects true execution status and contractual terms.

Performance Management & Continuous Improvement

Define and track key Controls service KPIs including:POC accuracy and forecast reliabilityBacklog aging and conversion rateInvoice cycle timeRevenue leakage and reworkLead structured problem-solving to address systemic execution issues.Drive continuous improvement using lean, standard work, and operational excellence principles.
Cross-Functional Alignment

Work closely with Controls Service Operations, Project Management, Finance, IT, and Commercial teams.Ensure execution systems support scalable Controls growth and digital service expansion.Align operational processes with customer delivery expectations and long-term service lifecycle objectives.

What We Look For

Senior leadership experience in operations, project operations, or systems governance within a global, multi-country environment.Strong understanding of project execution, commissioning workflows, and service lifecycle management.Deep expertise in Order-to-Cash processes and Percentage of Completion Accounting.Proven ability to design and enforce SOPs and standard work at scale.Strong influencing skills in matrix organizations with indirect leadership accountability.Highly analytical, data-driven, and execution-focused leadership style.

data.title: Consultants/Managing Consultants/Associate Director - FACD
data.jd_text: HKA is one of the world’s leading consultancies of choice for multi-disciplinary expert and specialist services in dispute resolution within, among others, the capital projects and infrastructure sector. We also advise clients on the economic and financial impact of commercial and investment treaty disputes and in forensic accounting matters. The success of our Forensic Accounting & Commercial Damages (FACD) practice and our vision for the future has seen the practice grow from strength to strength, with the addition of new partners and colleagues. At HKA, we don’t just offer jobs—we offer careers with purpose, growth, and global impact. As part of our Forensic Accounting & Commercial Damages (FACD) team, you’ll be joining a collaborative, high-performing group that’s shaping the future of dispute resolution and valuation worldwide. As Part Of The HKA Team You Can Expect Global Collaboration: Work alongside talented colleagues from across the globe, sharing insights and solving complex challenges together. Supportive Leadership: Our senior team is committed to mentoring and developing your career—your growth is our priority. Meaningful Work: Dive into high-profile valuation and damages engagements that span industries and continents. The Opportunity Our Forensic Accounting and Commercial Damages (FACD) practice in the UAE continues to thrive, experiencing significant growth and rapid expansion throughout 2025. As a result, we are seeking an experienced forensic accountant to join our team. Role This is an opportunity to work on disputes, forensic accounting, investigations and valuations projects across the MENA region in a variety of industries. In this role, you will be doing the following: Performing detailed financial analysis and investigations, and developing financial models from transactional and financial data. Performing business valuations using cutting-edge tools and data. Drafting expert report sections. Presenting findings to partners and clients. Engaging directly with clients. Supporting business development and attending networking events. Helping train and develop colleagues. If you’re ready to build on what you’ve enjoyed so far, explore new areas, and take your career in a direction that plays to your strengths—let’s talk. Skills And Experience Ideally you will possess the following skills and experience: A graduate or post-graduate in accounting, finance, economics, or business/management. A Qualified Accountant, CFA or equivalent with at least 2 years’ experience in a consulting environment (e.g. Forensic Services, Valuations, Transaction Services, Audit). Ideally, experience in Forensic Accounting or Contentious Valuations. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail. Strategic thinking and comfort with ambiguity. Excellent written and verbal communication. Native Arabic speaker is a plus. Willingness to travel across the GCC and Europe at short notice. What We Offer At HKA we recognise that our people are critical to our ongoing success. Our priority is to provide an inclusive and collaborative culture aimed at providing the best opportunities for our people to thrive and develop in their chosen career path. We operate a flexible working pattern both in terms of hours and location and are happy with flexible remote working. Our Global Recruitment team is committed to providing an authentic candidate experience for all those who take the time to apply to HKA. We are committed to providing an efficient and insightful recruitment process giving candidates a true reflection of life at HKA. ED&I At HKA HKA has a thriving ED&I committee working hard to provide an inclusive and welcoming environment for a truly diverse set of employees. We are committed as a business on the continued improvement of our Equality, Diversity and Inclusion strategy, please visit our careers page for further information or feel free to ask our recruitment team for further information. All HKA employment decisions are firstly made on merit and shall be made without regard to age, disability, gender or gender reassignment, marriage, and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex, and sexual orientation, or any other basis in compliance with local legislation. HKA is one of the world’s leading consultancy of choice for multi-disciplinary expert and specialist services in risk mitigation and dispute resolution within the capital projects and infrastructure sector. We also have experience advising clients on the economic impact of commercial and investment treaty disputes and in forensic accounting matters. As trusted independent consultants, experts and advisors, we deliver solutions amid uncertainty, dispute and overrun, and provide the insights that make the best possible outcomes a reality for public and private sector clients worldwide.

data.title: Project Director
data.jd_text: Egis is an international player active in architecture, consulting, construction engineering and mobility services. We create and operate intelligent infrastructures and buildings that respond to the climate emergency and contribute to more balanced, sustainable and resilient territorial development. Operating in 100 countries, Egis puts the expertise of its 19,500 employees at the service of its clients and develops cutting-edge innovations accessible to all projects. Through its wide range of activities, Egis is a key player in the collective organisation of society and the living environment of citizens all over the world. With 3,500 employees across 8 countries in the Middle East, Egis has delivered over 700 complex development projects, stimulating economic growth and enhancing quality of life. Ranked among the top ten firms in the Middle East by Engineering News Record (ENR), Egis is committed to sustainable development. The Group’s operations in the Middle East are built on strategic acquisitions and a deep understanding of local market conditions. Egis’ long history of providing comprehensive engineering, consulting, and project management services makes it a trusted partner for regional governments, investors, and developers. Job Description Project: Rail Project Civil Package Location: Dubai, United Arab Emirates Contract Type: Design & Build 1. Position Overview We are seeking a Project Director with proven experience in delivering Detailed Design for a project based in Jordan. The position will be contracted in the UAE, with travel to Jordan required as per client requests. The Project Director will lead and oversee the full design scope of the project, from detailed design through to IFC issuance, ensuring full contractual compliance and alignment with construction requirements, schedule, and budget objectives. Key Responsibilities Leadership & Governance Provide overall leadership and strategic direction for the design scope. Define and implement the design management strategy in line with contractual obligations. Establish and lead the integrated design organization (internal teams and external consultants). Contract & Risk Management Ensure full compliance of all deliverables with the Design & Build contract requirements. Identify, anticipate, and manage technical and contractual risks related to design. Oversee variations, change management processes, and support claims strategy as needed. Technical Oversight Supervise all design disciplines, including alignment, civil works, structures, viaducts, stations, rail systems, E&M, and interfaces. Validate key technical decisions and ensure multidisciplinary consistency. Manage complex technical interfaces between systems and construction packages. Schedule & Performance Management Lead the design schedule in a highly constrained delivery environment. Ensure adherence to milestones, budget, and quality standards. Implement robust reporting and performance monitoring mechanisms. Client & Stakeholder Management Act as the primary point of contact for the Client on all design-related matters. Participate in high-level coordination meetings with authorities and key stakeholders. Represent the project at executive and institutional levels. Team Leadership Lead and mentor discipline leads and design managers. Foster a high-performance, collaborative, and multicultural team environment. Ensure strong coordination with construction and site-based teams. Qualifications Master’s degree in Civil Engineering, Railway Engineering, or related field. Additional qualifications in Project Management (e.g., MBA, PMP) are an advantage. Experience Minimum 15 years of experience in large-scale infrastructure projects. Proven experience in major railway projects. Demonstrated experience delivering projects under Design & Build contracts. Prior experience in the Middle East is desirable. Technical Competencies Strong expertise in multidisciplinary design management. In-depth understanding of railway systems and infrastructure interfaces. Solid experience in risk management, planning, and complex project delivery. Leadership Skills Strong leadership and decision-making capabilities. Excellent communication and negotiation skills. Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, high-visibility international environment. Languages Fluent English required.
