data.title: Head - Year - Mamoura British Academy (AY 26/27)
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: Job Description Aldar Academies are currently seeking an outstanding Teacher - Primary with responsibility for Head of Year for Mamoura British Academy in Abu Dhabi commencing August 2026. Mamoura British Academy is a British curriculum, mixed gender school until Year 8 and a girl’s only school from Year 9 onwards. It is located in a state of the art building in the heart of Abu Dhabi. We strive for excellence in all that we do through our high expectations and commitment to ensuring that we provide high quality learning opportunities for our students. Our vision is to be ‘a sustainable and innovative learning community, where we nurture all learners through our bespoke Mamoura British Academy DNA .’ This Mamoura Learner, Teacher and Values DNA is at the heart of the school community. This is an exciting opportunity to join the highly successful Aldar family of schools and to help shape the future of Abu Dhabi’s leading school group. The successful candidates will be closely aligned with our core values and ethos and possess a genuine understanding and appreciation of the values of the region. Responsibilities Qualities and attributes: A supportive and approachable team player who can collaborate effectively with others. A good communicator at all levels. A commitment to developing positive relationships with all members of the school community. A desire to be part of a school that strives for excellence in all that we do. Passion to instil awe and wonder to the learning of young students. Leadership: Contribute to the whole school vision and the strategic direction of the school. Lead team and planning meetings to ensure that the team is well informed, organised and successful. Ensure that the learning opportunities for the children are innovative, purposeful and challenging across all curriculum areas. Promote the pastoral care and well-being of children within the year group by ensuring all systems and procedures are followed. Effectively line manage a team of teachers and teaching assistants to ensure they are performing successfully against the UAE Professional Teacher Standards. Contribute to the school’s Monitoring, Evaluating and Reporting processes and quality assure all aspects of provision within the year group to ensure we are consistently promoting high standards and expectations. Contribute to the school’s Professional Development cycle helping to upskill and share good practice amongst fellow teachers. Actively participate in the school’s bespoke Middle Leader training programme. Teaching and learning: To be able to teach all areas of the Primary English National Curriculum. Organising the classroom, learning resources and creating displays to encourage a positive learning environment and support the development of the Mamoura British Academy USP in this area. Planning, preparing and presenting lessons that cater for the needs of the whole ability range within the class. Providing support and interventions for students with EAL or who have additional learning needs. Motivating pupils with enthusiastic, imaginative presentation. Evidencing children’s work and learning in line with the Mamoura British Academy Marking and Feedback policy. Being reflective of lessons taught and student progress, and adjusting planning, curriculum and continuous provision to reflect this. Meeting with other professionals such as speech and language therapists, occupational therapists and educational psychologists, if required. Ensuring that the Mamoura British Academy Teacher and Learner DNA language and attributes are being embedded throughout the curriculum. Contribute to the development of teaching and learning across the school by sharing best practice, participating in school CPD and T&L initiatives. To be able to demonstrate effective use of technology through innovative and creative lesson delivery and continuous provision. Assessment and data: Taking responsibility for the attainment and progress of a class of children to ensure that they reach above expected expectations. Meeting the requirements for the assessment and recording of pupils' development using Learning Ladders. Using data provided by assessments to influence further planning and interventions. Providing regular feedback to parents and carers on a student’s progress at Parent Teacher Conferences and other meetings. To plan, prepare and contribute to termly Pupil Progress meetings. Behaviour management and duties: To follow the Mamoura British Academy behaviour management policy and promote the TRACK Values to set high expectations. Manage classes effectively, using approaches which are appropriate to pupils’ needs in order to inspire, motivate and challenge pupils. Maintain good relationships with pupils and parents and follow the school’s procedures to manage and challenge any rare occurrences of unwanted behaviour. Be a positive role model and demonstrate consistently the positive attitudes, values and behaviour which are expected of pupils

data.title: Principal Solution Implementation Manager
data.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.jd_text: At Anaplan, we are a team of innovators focused on optimizing business decision-making through our leading AI-infused scenario planning and analysis platform so our customers can outpace their competition and the market. What unites Anaplanners across teams and geographies is our collective commitment to our customers’ success and to our Winning Culture. Our customers rank among the who’s who in the Fortune 50. Coca-Cola, LinkedIn, Adobe, LVMH and Bayer are just a few of the 2,400+ global companies who rely on our best-in-class platform. Our Winning Culture is the engine that drives our teams of innovators. We champion diversity of thought and ideas, we behave like leaders regardless of title, we are committed to achieving ambitious goals, and we love celebrating our wins – big and small. Supported by operating principles of being strategy-led, values-based and disciplined in execution, you’ll be inspired, connected, developed and rewarded here. Everything that makes you unique is welcome; join us and let’s build what’s next - together! Strategic Consulting Meets Expert Delivery: Lead Anaplan's Enterprise Implementations Are you a seasoned consultant with a passion for seeing strategy through to flawless execution? We are looking for a Principal Solution Implementation Manager to lead Anaplan’s most complex and strategic customer engagements across the Middle East. This role is a unique hybrid of high-end consulting and hands-on program leadership. You will be the senior-most delivery advisor for our most important enterprise accounts, acting as the bridge between the customer’s strategic vision and the technical reality of implementation. You won’t just manage projects; you’ll serve as a trusted partner to C-level stakeholders, guiding them through large-scale business transformation. Based in our Abu Dhabi office with hybrid flexibility, you will be instrumental in shaping the success of iconic brands by delivering sophisticated, high-impact planning solutions. Your Impact Act as the lead strategic advisor for a portfolio of enterprise-level Anaplan deployments, translating complex business challenges into scalable solutions. Provide expert counsel to customer executives on governance, change management, project scoping, and long-term value realisation. Build and maintain C-suite relationships, operating as a trusted, non-sales partner focused entirely on the customer’s success. Orchestrate the end-to-end program, steering internal teams, partner consultants, and customer stakeholders toward delivery excellence. Command complex programs, mastering scope, risk, and budget to ensure predictable, high-quality outcomes. Intervene and recover at-risk projects, applying your advisory and leadership skills to navigate challenges and bring deployments back on track. Your Qualifications A background in management consulting, technology consulting, or a client-facing professional services role is essential. Multiple years of experience leading large-scale enterprise program delivery and execution, ideally from within a consultancy or a SaaS professional services organisation. ERP or EPM expertise in at least one of the following: Supply Chain, Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A), or Workforce Planning. Demonstrated ability to operate as a strategic advisor, with outstanding influencing and negotiation skills at the executive level. A track record of successfully delivering complex, multi-year initiatives against tight timelines and budgets. A resilient, adaptable, and proactive mindset, with a passion for solving complex business problems. Fluent communication skills in English and Arabic Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) We believe attracting and retaining the best talent and fostering an inclusive culture strengthens our business. DEIB improves our workforce, enhances trust with our partners and customers, and drives business success. Build your career in a place where diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging aren’t just words on paper – this is what drives our innovation, it’s how we connect, and it contributes to what makes us a market leader. We believe in a hiring and working environment where all people are respected and valued, regardless of gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, religion, ethnicity, age, neurodiversity, disability status, citizenship, or any other aspect which makes people unique. We hire you for who you are, and we want you to bring your authentic self to work every day! We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, perform essential job functions, and receive equitable benefits and all privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation. Fraud Recruitment Disclaimer It has come to our attention that fraudulent and fictitious job opportunities are being circulated on the Internet. Prospective candidates are being contacted

data.title: Regional CEO
data.jd_text: Job Description: The Regional CEO will serve as the strategic leader responsible for overseeing the operational, financial, and cultural success of the organization across multiple territories. This executive will be tasked with developing and executing comprehensive strategies that align with the overall vision of the organization, while fostering innovation and sustainable growth. The Regional CEO will leverage deep market insights to identify opportunities for expansion, ensuring competitive positioning within the industry. With a keen focus on building high-performance teams, the incumbent will lead efforts in transformation and change management, driving engagement and accountability at all levels. The ideal candidate will exemplify exemplary leadership qualities, superior communication skills, and a strong track record in stakeholder management, with the ability to cultivate relationships with partners, clients, and employees to enhance organizational reputation and profitability. Job Requirements: A minimum of 15 years of executive leadership experience, with at least 5 years in a CEO or equivalent C-suite role within a multinational organization. Proven track record in driving revenue growth and strategic vision implementation in complex, dynamic market environments. Demonstrated expertise in financial management, including forecasting, budgeting, and financial analysis, with a history of optimizing resource allocation to bolster profitability. Extensive experience in leading transformational initiatives and cultural change within diverse regions, ensuring alignment with corporate values and objectives. Exceptional stakeholder management skills with an ability to influence and engage a wide range of audiences, from internal teams to clients and board members. Advanced understanding of regulatory frameworks and compliance requirements pertinent to operations across various jurisdictions. History of establishing and sustaining high-performance teams, with a clear focus on talent development, employee engagement, and organizational effectiveness. Strategic thinker with a data-driven approach to decision making, adept at leveraging analytics to forecast trends and inform business strategies. Outstanding negotiation and conflict-resolution skills, with a proven ability to drive consensus and collaboration among diverse groups. Job Responsibilities: Develop and implement strategic plans for the region, ensuring alignment with the organization’s overall mission and goals. Oversee all operational activities, creating an organizational structure that maximizes efficiency and effectiveness across all functions. Build and lead a strong executive team, fostering a culture of excellence, accountability, and innovative thinking. Spearhead market analysis initiatives to identify growth opportunities and adjust strategies accordingly to maintain competitive advantage. Manage relationships with key stakeholders, including board members, partners, and regulatory bodies, ensuring transparency and alignment of interests. Establish and monitor performance metrics, driving accountability within the organization and ensuring achievement of financial and operational targets. Lead crisis management efforts, effectively communicating with the organization and external stakeholders during times of uncertainty. Champion sustainable development and corporate social responsibility initiatives that align with the organization’s values and enhance community engagement. Execute talent management strategies that attract, retain, and nurture high-caliber executives and teams throughout the region. Required Skills: Strategic Leadership: Ability to formulate and communicate a clear strategic vision that inspires and motivates others. Financial Acumen: Strong understanding of financial statements, metrics, and principles to drive informed decision-making. Change Management: Skilled in leading organizational change efforts, utilizing frameworks and methodologies that facilitate smooth transitions. Communication Excellence: Outstanding verbal and written communication skills with the ability to tailor message delivery to various audiences. Relationship Building: Capacity to cultivate and maintain effective relationships with diverse stakeholders at all levels. Negotiation Skills: Advanced proficiency in negotiation strategies that lead to mutually beneficial outcomes. Analytical Thinking: Aptitude for leveraging data analytics to assess performance and inform strategic direction. Cultural Competence: Deep understanding of cultural dynamics and their implications on business operations across different regions. Conflict Resolution: Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to resolve disputes and drive collaboration among differing viewpoints.

data.title: Vice President MMS META
data.jd_text: Job Description Summary We are the makers of possible BD is one of the largest global medical technology companies in the world. Advancing the world of health™ is our Purpose, and it’s no small feat. It takes the imagination and passion of all of us—from design and engineering to the manufacturing and marketing of our billions of MedTech products per year—to look at the impossible and find transformative solutions that turn dreams into possibilities. Why join us? A career at BD means learning and working alongside inspirational leaders and colleagues who are equally passionate and committed to fostering an inclusive, growth-centered, and rewarding culture. You will have the opportunity to help shape the trajectory of BD while leaving a legacy at the same time. To find purpose in the possibilities, we need people who can see the bigger picture, who understand the human story that underpins everything we do. We welcome people with the imagination and drive to help us reinvent the future of health. At BD, you’ll discover a culture in which you can learn, grow and thrive. And find satisfaction in doing your part to make the world a better place. Become a maker of possible with us! Our vision for Medication Management Solutions at BD A key and growing part of our business is Medication Management Solutions (MMS), which offers integrated solutions that touch all stages of the patient journey, with the goal of reducing errors and inefficiencies and increasing patient safety. Whether you join from a clinical, technical or innovation background, a career with us will help deliver a new healthcare environment and give a better experience for patients and those who provide them care. Overview Job Description The Vice President MMS META is a supervisory role reporting directly to the President META with close alignment to the MMS WW President. This role is responsible for leading, developing and implementing Marketing, Sales, and Support strategies for the MMS Business Unit for the META market. The VP responsibilities will include setting and driving the three-year strategic, operational and financial plan for MMS, setting high-reaching financial targets and achieving profitable growth, above market growth, conducting new market / opportunity analysis and building and engaging a highly effective team. The VP will be a member of the META Leadership Team and of the Global MMS Leadership Team Key Responsibilities Develop and maintain a solid understanding of the META market including future trends and the competitive landscape. Develop a strategic understanding / relationships with key customers. Effectively engage network of key influencers to drive strategic health care policy directions Responsible for driving the annual MMS META operating plan including meeting sales, gross profit and key financial metrics. Collaborate with Global Platform Leaders and Country General Managers to define new opportunities for entry into BD’s product development funnel and ensure commercial excellence for new product launches. Work closely with peer regional VPs to support country organizations to implement strategic priorities as defined under the regional Commercial Excellence program. Drive rigorous discipline in sales excellence and adherence to SFDC. Partners with leaders across platforms, regions, R&D, manufacturing, quality, regulatory, and finance to understand, develop and implement the strategic agenda through operational planning, process development and implementation of key projects. Supervise regional product pricing corridors, guidelines and policies for products sold in the META markets, in close alignment with Country Business Leaders to ensure regional pricing consistency and local competitiveness. Develop and implement the interpersonal capability plan in support of the strategy, crafting a highly customer centric organization demonstrated via personal leadership. Responsible for strengthening associate and leadership engagement in the MMS META organization. Identify acquisition targets in the region that can potentially have an immediate, transparent impact on BD's profit margin and growth rate in the region. Experiences Meaningful experience in Sales and Marketing, including proven P&L management experience, preferably in the medical devices' market. Proficiency in marketing, selling and new business development skills. Knowledge And Skills Preferred Knowledge and Experience Previous working experience with general management either in global, regional or country business leadership role Consistent track record of business / commercial success in a health care environment Proven knowledge of META healthcare system, environment and its key partners Experience in marketing innovation, market access and business development Experience in organizational change and change management Experience and/or capability to collaborate optimally with external leaders and key influencers Solid understanding across multiple functiona

data.title: Transition Portfolio Manager, Vice President, State Street Investment Management
data.jd_text:
Who We Are Looking For

We are seeking candidates with 10+ years of experience to join our Transition Management (TM) team in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In this role, you’ll work within State Street Investment Management’s global TM business, delivering transition and interim exposure management solutions to institutional investors worldwide. You’ll support and manage complex transitions across multiple asset classes, including equities, fixed income, FX, and exchange‑traded derivatives, serving clients domiciled in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia while contributing to the firm’s broader global transition management platform, including interim exposure management and reporting and analytics.

This role can be performed in a hybrid model, requiring at least 4 days of work in the office.

Why this role is important to us

State Street Investment Management (SSIM) is a global asset manager partnering with some of the world’s most sophisticated investors. Backed by State Street’s scale and stability, SSIM offers employees the opportunity to work on complex, high‑impact investment solutions in a collaborative, forward‑looking environment—where expertise, partnership, and long‑term thinking truly matter

This role will enable local delivery of our industry-leading TM service to clients domiciled in the KSA, and enhance our global coverage model by adding TM analysts in the Middle East. Expanding our global presence aligns with our continued commitment to providing the highest level of service to our clients.

What You Will Be Responsible For

As a Transition Portfolio Manager, you will:

Utilize your in-depth understanding of the transition life cycle (pre-trade, implementation, and post-trade aspects) to lead portfolio restructures for KSA-domiciled clients including complex multi-manager, multi-asset class events.Support KSA-domiciled clients and our global transition desk with pre- and post-trade analysis and execution reporting; planning and project management including communication across parties (clients, custodians, record-keepers, investment managers and consultants); portfolio management and order generation; collaboration with trading desk on trade strategy and instructions; and monitoring of daily middle office support functions (trade communications, reconciliations, corporate actions).Cover trade reconciliation process between internal systems for accuracy and completeness as needed prior to sending to middle office and custodians to ensure timely settlement of trades.Represent transition and interim exposure management products as a subject matter expert on multi-asset agency trading, exposure management, and hedging strategies.Apply your expertise on benchmarking and measurement of transition and trade execution performance through Transaction Cost Analysis (TCA)Support sales and business development initiatives and contribute to thought leadership on transition, exposure management and trading topicsAssess internal business operations (trading strategies, performance, technology, data management, marketing, research) and develop solutions to enhance product positioningLead and contribute to strategic projects in partnership with other groups / businesses within State Street as well as external vendorsAssist in evolving and industrializing our suite of trading, hedging and exposure analysis tools – including ongoing enhancement of pre- and post-trade analytics and interim exposure management platform – working with our Exposure Solutions team, reporting and analytics specialists, and external vendors


What We Value

The following skills will help you succeed in this role:

Portfolio management, trading and/or operational experience, preferably across multiple asset classesFamiliarity with Charles River Development, Axioma, or other portfolio and order management systems / toolsClient-facing experience leading complex transitions or portfolio implementations; strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skillsTechnical proficiency, with experience using Bloomberg and other market-data sources for analytical purposesSelf-starter with ability to work and problem solve independently under tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment, all while maintaining a critical focus on detailsStrong project management and organizational skills; pro-active in seeking out the next project to help the team


Education & Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, mathematics, economics, finance or similar10+ years of experience in a portfolio management, trading, consulting, analytical or operational roleMBA, graduate degree in a quantitative discipline, or CFA designation preferred


Additional Requirements

CMA registration requiredWillingness to travel up to 10% of the timeExperience with TCA, AI, programming languages, modeling in Excel / VBA and Python, data visualization software, and database infrastructures a plus


About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to

data.title: Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text: We are seeking a highly experienced Chief Financial Officer to lead financial strategy, governance, and performance management across a growing, multi entity organisation. This is a senior leadership role with direct interaction with the CEO and executive management, offering the opportunity to shape financial strategy and drive sustainable growth. The ideal candidate will bring strategic financial leadership with multi-unit oversight, advanced financial modelling and performance management skills, a strong focus on governance, compliance, and risk management, and executive level communication and stakeholder management. High ethical standards and integrity are essential. This role offers the chance to lead the finance function at a senior, strategic level, shape financial governance and controls, work with a dynamic leadership team driving transformation, and benefit from competitive compensation with performance based incentives. Please note this position is open to Saudi nationals only. Key Responsibilities: Develop and implement corporate financial strategy aligned with organisational objectives. Lead budgeting, forecasting, and long-term financial planning for multiple business units. Oversee financial control, internal governance, and compliance across the organisation. Monitor P&L performance, identify optimisation opportunities, and enhance profitability. Manage treasury, cash flow, and working capital to support operational and strategic goals. Provide financial analysis and scenario modelling to inform executive decision-making. Lead, mentor, and develop finance, accounting, and treasury teams. Support expansion initiatives, feasibility studies, and investment evaluation. Qualifications & Experience: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field; MBA preferred. Professional certification (CPA, ACCA, CMA, or equivalent) required. Minimum 15 years of progressive financial leadership experience, ideally in multi-entity organisations. Strong experience in financial control, governance, and risk management. Proven track record in leading executive finance teams and delivering strategic insights.

data.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) - Energy Sector
data.jd_text: Engagement details: Job Title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) - Energy Sector Client: Prominent business conglomerate Employment type: Permanent Location: Onsite in Dammam, Saudi Arabia Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering with an MBA is preferred Experience level: 15+ years Role Overview: Our client, a prominent business conglomerate, is seeking an experienced Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead the Industrial & Energy Division’s operations across KSA and Bahrain. The role carries overall operational responsibility for diverse business activities in machinery distribution, leasing, and energy products & services, with a strong mandate to achieve revenue and profit targets while driving the transformation from trading into a services- and local value–addition–led sustainable business model. Key Responsibilities: Design, plan, and implement business objectives, operational procedures, and staff structures for the Industrial & Energy Division. Set comprehensive goals for business growth and success, ensuring alignment with the division’s strategic business plans. Establish and enforce policies and procedures that promote company culture, vision, and compliance with group guidelines. Oversee daily operations across machinery distribution, leasing, and energy products & services, providing direction to divisional general managers and executive teams. Lead employees by example, fostering a performance-driven, customer-focused, and collaborative culture. Evaluate business performance by analyzing and interpreting financial and operational data, metrics, and KPIs. Create regular reports and presentations for the CEO and Executive Committee, covering strategic implementation, financial performance, and operational progress. Take the lead in business expansion activities, including new business lines, offerings, and regional growth within KSA and Bahrain, and support the CEO in executing strategic plans. Maintain and strengthen relationships with key customers, principals, partners, and vendors, including senior management teams of key accounts. Ensure positive cash contribution to the group through focused management of stocks, receivables, and working capital. Continuously evaluate and improve core processes against industry best practices across planning and budgeting, workforce management, operations, customer satisfaction, and staff performance. Drive succession planning for key positions, maintain prescribed localization levels in each country of operation, and attract and retain local talent. Key Skills and Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Chemical Engineering with an MBA is preferred. Minimum 15+ years of experience providing products and services into core sector industries such as energy, oil & gas, power & utilities, construction, mining, and manufacturing within the GCC region, with more than 10 years in an operational leadership position in a diverse business environment. KSA experience is preferred. Proven ability to run a large, independent profit centre with diverse business activities, including responsibility for revenue, profit, and cash flow objectives. Strong leadership, people management, and execution skills, with experience leading senior managers and cross-functional teams. Demonstrated capability in strategic planning, budgeting, and forecasting, as well as in implementing and monitoring KPIs and MIS for decision-making. Track record in business expansion, customer relationship management, and developing close working relationships with senior stakeholders, customers, and principals. Strong analytical thinking skills, perseverance, people management skills, high emotional intelligence, and a consistently customer-focused approach.

data.title: Chief Revenue Officer – E-Sports & Mega-Events
data.jd_text: Job Description: The Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) – E-Sports & Mega-Events is a pivotal executive role responsible for establishing comprehensive revenue generation strategies that align with the overarching business objectives of the organization. The CRO will lead the development and execution of innovative business models tailored to capitalize on current and emerging trends in the e-sports and mega-events sectors. This role demands a visionary leader with a proven track record in driving revenue growth, utilizing market insights to inform strategic decisions, and forging impactful partnerships that elevate the organization's presence and profitability within the competitive landscape. The successful candidate will demonstrate exceptional leadership capabilities, able to coordinate cross-functional teams to optimize sales performance, enhance customer engagement, and ensure the effective promotion of our brand at key events. Job Requirements: A minimum of 15 years of executive-level experience in revenue generation and sales strategy within the e-sports, entertainment, or related industries. Proven ability to develop and implement comprehensive revenue strategies that significantly boost financial performance. Exceptional understanding of market dynamics, consumer behavior, and competitive landscape specific to e-sports and mega-events. Demonstrated experience in establishing and nurturing high-value partnerships to enhance revenue streams and brand visibility. Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and drive business decisions based on insights. Foster a culture of continuous improvement and innovation within the sales team to meet organizational goals. Job Responsibilities: Design and execute a multi-faceted revenue generation strategy focusing on e-sports and mega-events that aligns with the company’s strategic objectives. Oversee all aspects of revenue operations, including sales forecasting, pipeline management, and budget allocation to maximize profitability. Lead cross-functional collaboration among marketing, product development, and sales teams to ensure cohesive strategies and efficient execution. Build and manage relationships with key stakeholders, partners, and sponsors to create new revenue opportunities and enhance brand partnerships. Monitor industry trends and competitor activities to identify growth opportunities and inform strategic adjustments. Establish performance metrics that drive accountability and continuous improvement within the revenue generation teams. Act as a spokesperson for the organization at industry events, effectively representing the organization’s vision and revenue strategies. Required Skills: Strategic thinking with strong business acumen to identify new revenue opportunities in the dynamic e-sports landscape. Advanced negotiation and communication skills for effective partnership development and stakeholder engagement. Excellent leadership abilities to inspire and influence cross-functional teams towards achieving revenue goals. Proficiency in data analytics and business intelligence tools for informed decision-making and performance tracking. Strong budgetary and financial management skills to ensure allocated resources are utilized effectively for revenue growth. Ability to foster a collaborative team environment that encourages innovative thinking and new approaches to revenue generation. Results-oriented mindset with a passion for achieving and exceeding ambitious business targets in a fast-paced environment.

data.title: Inverto | Business & Operations Senior Manager
data.jd_text: Who We Are At Inverto in Riyadh, we’re defining what’s next in procurement and supply chain—driven by ambition, precision, and impact. With the strength of BCG behind us and a sharp entrepreneurial mindset at our core, we partner with leading global organizations to deliver strategies that create lasting value. Our people are central to that mission. As experts who move with intent and momentum, we continually invest in developing our capabilities, expanding our reach, and scaling our impact. Growth at Inverto is shared growth-opening new opportunities for our clients, our business, and our teams. What You'll Do The Business & Operations Senior Manager drives the effective operationalization of Inverto’s regional growth agenda by leading key commercial, people, financial, and operational processes for the Middle East. Acting as a strategic partner to Inverto leadership and EMESA teams, the role ensures transparency in business development activities, enables strong go-to-market execution, and supports high-quality decision-making through robust performance and financial management. It plays a central role in shaping and sustaining talent and team development, driving a cohesive and high-performing culture, and ensuring optimal staffing and resource allocation. The position also safeguards operational excellence by strengthening cross-functional collaboration with BCG Core and Inverto EMESA, ensuring reliable data flow, and securing compliance with governance, legal, and internal standards. Additionally, it oversees local office operations and orchestrates shared services and marketing initiatives to enable seamless delivery and reinforce Inverto’s brand presence in the region. A key aspect of the role is ensuring alignment between BCG core and Inverto-specific requirements, in accordance with Inverto’s global guardrails, and business model. Overall, the role is critical in enabling the Middle East business to operate effectively, grow sustainably, and achieve its strategic objectives. data.title: Testing & Commissioning Section Head (HQ)
data.jd_text: Job Description The Testing & Commissioning Section Head (E2) is responsible for leading and managing all testing, validation, and commissioning activities across large-scale projects, ensuring full compliance with client requirements, operational standards, and regulatory obligations. The role oversees the preparation, review, and execution of commissioning plans, coordinates closely with contractors, engineers, operators, and authorities, and ensures all systems are properly integrated, tested, and ready for handover. With extensive experience, the position also drives risk management, stakeholder coordination, team leadership, and adherence to HSE standards while ensuring timely project completion, proper documentation, and successful transition to operations. Responsibilities Managing the integration and validation activities of the Contractor, together with the validation of all Test and Commissioning plans and test procedures for the program. Lead, direct and supervise a team of discipline Engineers in preparation and execution of commissioning of work Location. Assuming entire responsibility for the completion of the implementation in conformance with the client’s needs and project standards. Plan, organize and supervise works to ensure all works performed within the parameters of the project schedule. Liaison with the Systems Engineering team for engineering support and control. Coordinate with the Operator, Civil Defiance, EXPRO and other third parties related to all necessary testing and commissioning requirements for the project. Ensure all testing and commissioning works are comprehensive and discussed with stakeholders to ensure best implementation. Approval of testing and commissioning works. Participate in negotiations with regulatory agencies in public meetings with the client. Initiate risk assessment of testing and commissioning works, review and endorse risk assessment results and recommend course of action to ensure mitigation of risks. Attend to final inspections on completed projects and initiate Contract Completion Certificates and Handover Certificates Coordinates all activities for the successful Commissioning of the project within project schedules and contractual requirements. Oversees all coordination between Client, Contractor, vendors, Engineering, sub-contractors and any other external or internal agency to ensure the smooth Commissioning of the process plant. Review calibration of testing equipment on site. Reviewing the testing and commissioning plans submitted by the Contractor to ensure compliance with the operational requirements of the project. Writing Testing & Commissioning reports and documentation. Ensure completion of training for maintenance and operative staff, O&M manual, spare parts and as-built drawings. Informs themselves of the relevant Quality, Environmental, Safety and Occupational Health Policies, Manuals and Procedures within Hill International and ensures continued compliance with these requirements while employed by Hill. Perform other duties as assigned by the line manager/supervisor. Qualifications A degree in engineering or scientific discipline. Minimum 20 years’ experience in large-scale projects including having worked as the testing and commissioning manager. Knowledge of all phases of construction operations relating to field activities of engineering disciplines, construction technologies and jobsite management. Experience of implementing a zero accident philosophy. Extensive knowledge of health, safety and environmental policies, processes and procedures. Knowledge of the Project Construction Environmental Control Plan. Excellent knowledge of Water and Wastewater works.

data.title: Quality Assurance Section Head (HQ)
data.jd_text: Job Description The Quality Assurance Section Head (E2) is responsible for establishing, implementing, and monitoring project-wide quality assurance systems to ensure full compliance with corporate policies, international standards, and client requirements. The role involves developing quality plans, conducting audits, identifying trends and non-conformities, and driving corrective actions to ensure that all project deliverables meet defined quality standards. With extensive experience, the position also provides leadership in quality management, supports continuous improvement initiatives, and ensures effective reporting, training, and stakeholder coordination across all project phases. Responsibilities Responsible for monitoring the implementation of the Projects’ Management System in compliance with the Hill Corporate policies, International Standards and Client's requirements. Develops quality assurance plans by conducting hazard analyses and monitoring procedures. Implementation of the Quality System may require the development or modification of corporate procedures. As per project needs, the development of a Project-specific Quality Plan, as a roadmap of the Project Quality Requirements, is recommended. The Quality Assurance Manager monitors the establishment by the service provider of the quality assurance (QA) programs sufficient for the Project. Monitors and audits the quality-related activities set forth in the Quality Plans to confirm that products or services provided are in accordance with the client's requirements. Periodically conducts detailed audits to assess the level of compliance with the QA systems, procedures, and related quality activities. Regularly interacts with the Project Director and Area Manager, and other staff members to assess on-site activities. Identifies and reports to the Project Director and -as per needs- to the Area Manager and the Area Quality Manager on quality issues or trends based on data from a variety of sources. Supports implementation of appropriate corrective action to quality issues. Validates quality processes by establishing product specifications and quality attributes. Provides input to management on projects performance metrics and process or procedural system improvements. Trains and motivates employees to ensure that quality standards are met. Requires the ability to communicate clearly, and interface effectively with a diverse group of professionals. Prepares quality documentation and reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing information and trends. Informs themselves of the relevant Quality, Environmental, Safety and Occupational Health Policies, Manuals and Procedures in place within Hill International, and ensures continued compliance with these requirements while employed by Hill. Perform other duties as assigned by the line manager/supervisor. Qualifications Bachelor in engineering/management from an accredited university. Minimum 20 years’ professional experience including as quality manager for large-scale projects. Certified as a Lead Auditor. Broad knowledge of the quality principles/tools and their application to engineering, environmental, procurement, construction, testing, operations, or decommissioning. Knowledge of the application of corrective actions to reconcile nonconforming conditions. Knowledge of regulatory requirements and functions which impact the quality program/plan. Proficiency in verbal and written communications. Proficiency in providing direction and on the job training to assigned personnel. Experience in directing project audit activities

data.title: Principal Engagement Manager
data.jd_text: The Opportunity The Digital Strategy Group (DSG) EMEA is Adobe’s strategic advisory team. We partner with the world’s leading brands to shape their digital experience vision and accelerate business transformation. Working alongside Adobe’s enterprise sales teams, DSG engages directly with customer executives. We help define digital strategies, shape future customer experiences, and build compelling cases for transformation powered by Adobe’s multi-cloud platform. As a Principal Consultant, you will act as a trusted advisor to C-suite leaders across Regulated industries (financial services, insurance, pharma, healthcare & public sector). You will support complex digital transformation initiatives and help build Adobe’s most strategic enterprise opportunities. This is a high-impact individual contributor role that combines industry expertise, critical thinking and consultative engagement. You will collaborate with sales leaders, solution consultants and partners to help customers unlock the full value of Adobe and deliver meaningful business outcomes! What You’ll Do Strategic Advisory & Executive Engagement – Partner with C-suite leaders across Regulated Industries . Shape transformation strategies and lead executive discussions aligned to Adobe’s capabilities. Vision & Experience Strategy – Co-create “north star” customer experiences. Define the capabilities required to deliver them. Value & Business Case Development – Develop value-based narratives and quantified business cases. Demonstrate the economic impact of Adobe solutions. Deal Strategy & Opportunity Acceleration – Work with enterprise sales teams to shape complex opportunities. Position Adobe as a strategic transformation partner. Strategic Problem Solving – Strong analytical and critical thinking skills. Ability to structure complex problems and develop clear recommendations. Consultative Collaboration – Experience supporting complex enterprise opportunities. Skilled in value storytelling, crafting financial justifications, and cross-functional collaboration. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity. Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours. Let’s Adobe together At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture, focus on people, purpose and community, Adobe for All, comprehensive benefits programs, the stories we tell, the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create. Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. Learn more. Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. data.title: Chief Transformation Officer – Public Sector & Ministries
data.jd_text: Job Description: The Chief Transformation Officer (CTO) – Public Sector & Ministries will spearhead the strategic initiatives aimed at modernizing and enhancing the effectiveness and efficiency of public services within governmental and ministerial entities. This C-suite executive will be responsible for the comprehensive transformation agenda, leveraging innovative methodologies and frameworks to foster a culture of agility, collaboration, and outcome-oriented performance. The CTO will partner with key stakeholders, including senior officials and community leaders, to align transformation initiatives with organizational objectives and constituent needs, ensuring a seamless transition from legacy systems to integrated, technology-driven solutions. The role demands exceptional leadership capabilities to cultivate high-performing teams, drive stakeholder engagement, and promote an equitable and inclusive approach throughout the transformation process. The ideal candidate will embody a strong vision for public service excellence, facilitating a mindset shift that redefines the interface between government entities and the public. Job Requirements: Minimum of 15 years of progressive executive experience in public administration, transformation management, or related fields, preferably within the public sector. Proven track record in leading transformative initiatives at a C-suite level, demonstrating substantial impact on organizational performance, service delivery, and operational efficiency. Extensive knowledge of government policies, regulatory frameworks, and public sector dynamics that influence transformation efforts. Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information into actionable plans. Excellent communication skills, including the ability to articulate a compelling vision and effectively engage diverse stakeholder groups. Demonstrated competence in change management methodologies and the ability to lead organizations through periods of significant transition. Experience with data-driven decision-making, including the capability to leverage analytics and performance metrics to guide transformation initiatives. Strong interpersonal skills that foster collaboration and consensus-building across varied entities and community stakeholders. Proficiency in leading cross-functional teams in a matrix organizational structure, demonstrating adaptability to different working styles and team dynamics. Job Responsibilities: Develop and implement a comprehensive transformation strategy that aligns with the strategic objectives of public sector organizations and ministries. Collaborate with senior leadership to define and articulate the vision for transformation, ensuring that it resonates with internal and external stakeholders. Assess current operational frameworks and identify opportunities for improved processes, technologies, and resource allocations to enhance service delivery. Foster a culture of continuous improvement, innovation, and accountability throughout public sector organizations by championing best practices and emerging technologies. Engage with government officials, community leaders, and external partners to ensure stakeholder alignment on transformation goals and metrics. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of transformation initiatives through regular assessments, adjusting strategies as necessary to achieve desired outcomes. Act as a key spokesperson for transformation initiatives, representing the organization at public forums, conferences, and stakeholder meetings. Lead and mentor cross-functional teams, promoting professional development and building leadership capacities within the organization. Champion initiatives that promote equity, diversity, and inclusion throughout all transformation efforts, ensuring diverse perspectives are integrated into decision-making. Prepare and present regular status updates to the board and legislative bodies, highlighting progress, challenges, and strategic adjustments to transformation initiatives. Required Skills: Exceptional leadership skills with a proven ability to influence and inspire, leading large-scale organizational change. Strong expertise in transformation methodologies, including Agile, Lean Six Sigma, and other relevant frameworks. Advanced project management skills, with proficiency in facilitating complex projects from conception through execution and evaluation. High-level negotiation and conflict resolution skills to navigate diverse stakeholder interests and drive consensus. In-depth knowledge of emerging technologies relevant to public service transformation, including AI, data analytics, and digital platforms. Ability to create and sustain collaborative partnerships across government, non-profit, and private sectors for integrated transformation efforts. Financial acumen to manage budgets effectively and ensure resource allocation aligns with strategic priorities. Strong advocacy

data.title: Category Excellence & Customer Development OT Senior Manager
data.jd_text: Overview The Category Excellence Manager is responsible for leading and enhancing the category growth and activation agenda across Modern Trade, E-Commerce, and Away-From-Home (AFH) channels. The role adapts worldwide category management and customer engagements best practices to the KSA market, ensuring proper rollout and adoption. It leads the World Class Customer Engagement (WCCE) model, drives cross-functional alignment across Sales, RGM, Marketing and E-Com, and ensures execution excellence in major commercial events (e.g. World Cup, formula 1, Ramadan). The manager also supports capability building across field and head-office teams to deliver profitable, insight-led category growth. Responsibilities Develop the multi-channel category capability-building strategy, with a detailed annual rollout and scorecard. Partner with E-Commerce and AFH teams to embed category leadership principles and 4P strategies across all trade channels. Lead the development of customer-and event-based toolkits (assortment, content/visibility, pricing & promotions) Co-own national event planning (World Cup. Formula 1, Ramadan, national Day) with Marketing and Sales for unified execution. Ensure regular review and organization of KPIs across MT, E-Com, and AFH performance dashboards. Coach Key Account Managers on profitable account management, leveraging analytics and shopper insights. Collaborate with the RGM and OT Excellence teams to integrate pricing, pack, and promotional effectiveness in trade plans. Document post-initiative learnings and share best practices to strengthen category execution capabilities across teams. Qualifications 8-10 years of FMCG experience in Sales, Category Management, or Trade Marketing, with exposure to E-Commerce and/or AFH. Strong commercial awareness and understanding of channel economics, shopper behavior, and event-based execution. Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills with proficiency in EPOS, Nielsen, Power BI, and digital commerce dashboards. Demonstrated functional expertise and a proven track record of delivering results in complex multi-channel environments. Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to lead discussions and influence across all levels. Excellent relationship building and collaboration skills, with internal stakeholders (Sales, RGM, Marketing) and external partners (Retailers, Platforms, Distributors.) Project management capability-able to lead cross functional teams, build critical paths, and deliver initiatives on time. Strong organizational ability to manage a high workload, multiple priorities, and deliver consistent outcomes. Experience in event-based commercial planning (e.g. Ramadan, formula 1, World Cup). Fluent in English and Arabic with excellent presentation and facilitation skills. Strong interpersonal and leadership skills, able to motivate and develop teams while maintaining collaborative collaborative relationship across functions. Results-driven and strategically minded, demonstrating integrity, sound judgment, and discipline planning in execution.

data.title: HEAD, DIGITAL TECHNOLOGY
data.jd_text: Primary Purpose Of The Job Play a pivotal role in QatarEnergy’s digital transformation by leading the delivery of innovative digital solutions that optimize operations, enhance safety, and unlock new business value. As Head of Digital Technology, you will guide a dynamic team in designing and implementing cutting-edge Cloud AI, and analytics initiatives. You will work closely with business leaders, IT, and global technology partners to ensure digital excellence. By championing Agile methodologies, you will accelerate innovation, enable rapid delivery, and foster a culture of continuous improvement Required Experience And Skills Strategic Leadership: 15+ years of progressive experience in Oil & Gas (Operations, Production, Maintenance, Engineering, Drilling & Wells, Reservoir, or Geosciences), with at least 6 years in digital transformation roles. Agile Delivery: Demonstrated experience leading digital teams and programs using Agile methodologies, with a focus on rapid prototyping, iterative delivery, and measurable business outcomes. Digital Innovation: Proven success in leading large-scale digital programs, including Data Analytics, AI, and ML on the cloud within the energy sector. Change Management: Demonstrated ability to drive digital adoption, foster a culture of innovation, and lead cross-functional teams through change. Business Impact: Strong track record of identifying and delivering high-impact digital use cases with measurable ROI. Financial Acumen: Experience managing digital budgets, vendor relationships, and performance metrics. Industry Insight: Up-to-date knowledge of IoT, automation, cybersecurity, and sustainability trends in Oil & Gas. Collaboration & Communication: Exceptional interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and inspire stakeholders at all levels. Educational Qualifications Master’s degree in Science, Engineering, Computer Science, Oil & Gas, Energy Management, or a related field is preferred. Candidates with a Bachelor’s degree and extensive (15+ years) relevant experience will also be considered. Advanced degrees (Master’s or PhD) are highly desirable. Professional certifications in digital transformation, cloud, AI, Agile, or related technologies are an advantage.

data.title: Asset & Wealth Management - Doha - Vice President - Lead Security Engineering
data.jd_text: Job Description Role Overview The Lead Information Security Officer for Asset Management is a critical leadership position responsible for defining, implementing, and overseeing the comprehensive information security and cybersecurity risk posture specifically within the Asset Management Private business. This role is pivotal in balancing commercial objectives with robust security controls, ensuring the division's resilience against an evolving threat landscape, and protecting client assets and data. This leader will directly manage and provide strategic direction to teams responsible for Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC), Application Security & Advisory, and Product Security functions within Asset Management. Crucially, this role also involves the oversight and guidance of embedded Technology Risk Officers who are assigned to various Asset Management Private business vertical. The objective is to foster a unified and proactive approach to risk management, ensuring regulatory compliance, and enabling secure technological innovation across all Asset Management initiatives. Key Responsibilities Work with internal application development teams that are developing the next generation of critical business applications, enable them to understand Information Security and Business Resiliency control requirements, and advise on the integration of these controls into their applications Collaborate with the global Application Security Risk, Business Continuity, Risk Measurement, and other global Technology Risk teams to develop and integrate best-in-class security and resiliency controls and practices. Communicate the impact of technology risks and the approach to mitigation/acceptance and provide risk assessment and advisory services to technology engineers, and technology and business management. Assess existing applications for design-related security risks and assist teams in determining appropriate remediation steps Provide guidance to engineering leadership and application developers on existing and emerging threats in the web and mobile application space. Drive adoption of embedded application security controls as part of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). Provide deep subject matter expertise to application teams in secure application design and development approaches and techniques. Contribute to the technical understanding, adoption and convergence of information security standards, solutions and tools. Work with engineers to develop customized security testing strategy to complement the existing security testing program managed by Technology Risk. Skills And Experience Required Experience: 5+ years of progressive experience in Multi domain Information Security experience such as vendor security, application security, vulnerability management, data loss prevention, data encryption, and infrastructure security. Regulatory & Risk Expertise: Expert knowledge of global financial regulations (e.g., SEC , FINRA , GDPR , CCPA ) and proven experience applying risk management methodologies such as FAIR (Factor Analysis of Information Risk) or similar frameworks. Leadership & Management: Proven ability to build, mentor, and lead high-performing global teams of security professionals. Program Management: Proven track record involving collaboration with engineering, technology, second line risk functions and audit partners to deliver projects and facilitate resolution of audit issues within committed timelines. Communication: Exceptional written and oral communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical risks and solutions clearly to both technical and executive audiences. Risk Management: Expertise in performing risk assessments, identifying gaps in compliance with information security policies, and recommending effective mitigation strategies. Security Standards: Familiarity with leading security standards and frameworks such as NIST, OWASP, SANS Top 20, PCI DSS, and CIS Controls. Technical Depth: Expertise in Technology Risk data analytics (metrics reporting and dashboarding) and Reviewing Software Development Lifecycle best practices e.g., code reviews, vulnerability scan report analysis to advise application development teams on for secure practices and frameworks, and other application security best practices Preferred Qualifications BS or MS degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Security, or a related technical field. Relevant industry certifications such as CISSP , CISM , CRISC , CISA, or cloud-specific security certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Security – Specialty). Experience with leveraging AI/ML to solve security problems and scale operations. Knowledge of secure coding languages (e.g., Python, Java, Go). About Goldman Sachs At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. H

data.title: Group Chief Financial Officer
data.jd_text: Purpose The Chief Financial Officer (CFO) is responsible for the overall financial stewardship of Ooredoo Fintech, ensuring robust financial governance, regulatory compliance, and operational discipline across the organization, in a fast growing, multi country environment. The role supports the company’s continued growth and operational scale by maintaining strong financial controls, reliable reporting, and efficient financial operations. In addition, the CFO will play a key role in driving transformation within the finance function, strengthening processes, systems, and capabilities to support a maturing fintech platform and an evolving regulatory environment. This is a hands-on role suited to a leader with a builder mentality, highly structured, with a strong personal presence and gravitas. Context OFT is an innovative and dynamic scale-up fintech company. We offer the heritage and stability of a hugely successful founding group combined with the unlimited ambition and passion of a talented team building intuitive, secure and convenient money management and payment tools for everyday people. The company operates digital wallet and payment services, including remittances, card transactions, airtime top-ups, and payroll flows. Maintaining customer trust, regulatory licence adherence, and financial resilience is critical. The business currently operates in 4 countries (Qatar, Oman, Tunisia and Maldives) and it is building in 3 additional countries (Iraq, Algeria, Kuwait). The business is the most ambitious fintech business sin the MENA region and is run with a highly centralised operating model, and high reusability of centrally defined process, systems and procedures. Working with us presents a unique and exciting opportunity to make a difference and be part of a revolution where we are leveraging financial technology to provide accessible, empowering tools to consumer and merchants. Role Accountabilities 1. Financial Strategy & Business Partnership Define and lead the financial strategy of the business, ensuring full alignment with corporate objectives, growth ambitions, and regulatory expectations. Act as a trusted strategic advisor to the CEO and Executive Committee, providing forward-looking financial insight to support product innovation, market expansion, and capital deployment decisions Drive long-term financial planning, including capital allocation, funding strategy, and value creation across markets and product lines. 2. Financial Governance, Control & Reporting Establish and maintain a best-in-class financial control environment, ensuring full compliance with IFRS and all applicable local regulatory requirements. Oversee the timely and accurate delivery of management accounts, board reporting, statutory financial statements, and regulatory submissions Ensure audit readiness at all times, with robust governance frameworks, policies, and controls embedded across the organisation. Own the integrity, consistency, and transparency of all financial data and reporting. 3. Finance Transformation, Systems & Scalability Lead the transformation of the finance function into a scalable, technology-enabled, and data-driven organisation. Design and implement robust, end-to-end processes and control frameworks that support growth, multi-country operations, and increasing regulatory complexity. Champion automation, system optimisation, and advanced analytics to improve efficiency, accuracy, and decision-making capability. Partner closely with Product and Technology teams to ensure financial architecture, reconciliation tools, and data flows are fit for scale and future innovation. 4. Financial Operations & Liquidity Management Provide executive oversight of all financial operations, including reconciliations, settlement processes, escrow management, and partner funding flows. Ensure robust safeguarding of customer funds, strict adherence to settlement SLAs, and effective fund segregation practices Lead liquidity management, including cash forecasting, working capital optimisation, and foreign exchange exposure management. Maintain operational resilience across all financial processes, ensuring continuity under stress scenarios. 5. Regulatory Engagement & Compliance Serve as the primary finance interface with regulators, central banks, auditors, and external stakeholders. Ensure full compliance with all licensing conditions, regulatory reporting requirements, and fintech/payment services regulations. Oversee financial aspects of AML/FCC frameworks in collaboration with Risk and Compliance functions. Proactively manage regulatory relationships, anticipating changes and ensuring the organisation remains ahead of evolving requirements. 6. Performance Management & Value Creation Lead all FP&A activities, including budgeting, forecasting, and performance tracking across markets and products. Translate complex financial and transactional data into clear, actionable insights for senior management and the Board. Drive dis

data.title: HEAD, PROJECT CONTROLS & SERVICES
data.jd_text: Primary Purpose Of The Job Lead the Project Services team, including Senior Cost Engineer, Senior Planning Engineer, and Senior Document Controller, to deliver cost management, budget control, planning and scheduling, contracts administration, accounts payable, document control, project reporting, and general administrative services. Ensure the successful and timely execution of the FEED and EPIC stages for the Crude Oil Stabilisation, Zero routine flaring, and OFFRAG Pipeline Project, within budget and in line with project requirements. Required Experience And Skills Minimum 18 years’ experience in Oil & Gas, with at least 8 years in a senior project control role. Proficiency in Primavera P6, P6 Analytics, Primavera Risk Analysis, MS Project, MS Office, and SAP. Strong leadership, communication, conflict resolution, and presentation skills. Experience in major project execution, including engineering, procurement, fabrication, installation, commissioning, and start-up. Educational Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in engineering (master’s preferred). Membership in a recognized professional engineering association/institution. PMP certification is an advantage.

data.title: Head CCM Services and Lead BCO, Qatar
data.jd_text: Head CCM Services and Lead BCO, Qatar (53345530) GCB 4 Some careers open more doors than others. If you’re looking to unlock new job opportunities, look at the possibilities right on your doorstep here at HSBC. HSBC Corporate and Institutional Banking is a markets-led, financing-focused business that provides investment and financial solutions. Through our international network, we connect emerging and mature markets, covering key growth areas. We partner with our corporate, government and institutional clients to help them achieve consistent, long-term performance. Our products and services include advisory, financing, prime services, research and analysis, securities services, trading and sales and transaction banking. We are currently seeking a high calibre professional to join our team as a Country Head of CCM Services & Lead BCO In This Role, You Will Ensure CCM OKRs are achieved for their respective market Lead the execution and implementation of the BCO function, to efficiently manage the underwriting process across country CIB. The BCO function to be globally leading across the bank, leveraging organizational design capabilities to optimize processes and structures. Take a lead in managing the portfolio and risk exposure partnering with banking teams and WCRM to determine and monitor risk appetite Drive policy and procedure simplification / optimization to make the underwriting process more effective with a focus on value-add activities and creating risk differentiated processes Continuously focus on improving the productivity of the function whilst enhancing client experience Providing oversight to outsourced credit & capital management services Landing an ambitious multi-year transformation journey within their market Implementing the Target CCM Services Operating Model and delivering financial targets associated with its’ implementation in the market Leading risk and control management across the end-to-end process Manage CCM COO FRP, budgeting and planning processes for the market Land locally the transformation and digitisation of the CCM COO journey and lead local initiatives to improve and re-engineer processes for the market Monitor and analyse customer feedback, identifying key areas of focus to significantly improve customer outcomes for the market Represent CCM Services in front of market stakeholders and at market forum Unify and provide direction to a dislocated set of smaller teams, fostering a sense of unity and purpose and embed the Target CCM services operating model Develop and maintain a diverse, innovation-driven credit and lending workforce, ensuring high levels of engagement and satisfaction. Oversee and execute the delivery of CCM services in-country (where required), ensuring alignment with regional processes and a seamless and efficient process for customers Monitor and analyse customer feedback within the market, identifying local areas of focus to improve customer outcomes while ensuring alignment with regional and global priorities. Collaborate with global stakeholders, including technology, CCM, WCRM, Legal, the global business Coverage teams, digital channels and products to implement the overall strategy for the wholesale credit and lending journey. Implement governance frameworks to ensure risks and issues related to the Credit & Lending journey are escalated to regional, and global stakeholders and resolved in a satisfactory manner. Monitor the management of financial and non-financial risks pertaining to the CCM production in partnership with local NFR/Business Risk. Act as the local Business Service Owner for Credit & Lending across CIB, ensuring service resilience. Manage and execute local end-to-end processes for Credit & Lending on behalf of the CIB Business. Monitor and manage local risk and controls across the end-to-end controls, meeting all SOX obligations. Support the OKR, KPI and SLA management framework within the market, maintaining service management oversight pertaining to both operational and control performance across all CCM CTB & RTB services end-to-end. Collaborate with the CCMS Product, Design and Innovation Pillar, technology and transformation teams to execute the technology strategy for CCM, reduce legacy technologies, and drive a culture of innovation by developing cutting-edge automation, capability, and business solutions leveraging next-gen technologies, AI, and analytics. Manage multiple senior stakeholders with varied and sometimes conflicting agendas, effectively navigating complex situations and driving alignment. Maintain a diverse, innovation-driven credit and lending workforce, ensuring high levels of engagement and satisfaction. To be successful in the role, you should meet the following requirements: Extensive years of experience in wholesale credit and lending, with a strong background in coverage, credit management or operations Proven track record of leading and managing teams, with experience in organizational design and strat
